1: Happiness, blessedness. From 'ashar; happiness; only in masculine plural construction as interjection, how happy!
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Counsel, advice. From ya'ats; advice; by implication, plan; also prudence.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Sinful, sinners. Intensively from chata'; a criminal, or one accounted guilty.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
Or moshab; from yashab; a seat; figuratively, a site; abstractly, a session; by extension an abode; by implication, population.
A primitive root; properly, to make mouths at, i.e. To scoff; hence to interpret, or intercede.
2: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Delight, pleasure. From chaphets; pleasure; hence desire; concretely, a valuable thing; hence a matter.
Direction, instruction, law. Or torah; from yarah; a precept or statute, especially the Decalogue or Pentateuch.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Direction, instruction, law. Or torah; from yarah; a precept or statute, especially the Decalogue or Pentateuch.
To moan, growl, utter, speak, muse. A primitive root; to murmur; by implication, to ponder.
Daytime, by day. From yowm; daily.
Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.
3: To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Tree, trees, wood. From atsah; a tree; hence, wood.
To transplant. A primitive root; to transplant.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A channel, canal. From palag; a rill.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
Leaf, leafage. From alah; a leaf; collectively, foliage.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to wilt; generally, to fall away, fail, faint; figuratively, to be foolish or wicked; causatively, to despise, disgrace.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To push forward. Or tsaleach; a primitive root; to push forward, in various senses.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
4: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Chaff, threshed loose). Or mowts; from muwts; chaff (rather) threshed loose).
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To drive, drive asunder. A primitive root; to shove asunder, i.e. Disperse.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
5: Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
Sinful, sinners. Intensively from chata'; a criminal, or one accounted guilty.
Congregation. Feminine of ed in the original sense of fixture; a stated assemblage.
6: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.

1: Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
To be in tumult or commotion. A primitive root; to be tumultuous.
People. Or l owm; from an unused root meaning to gather; a community.
To moan, growl, utter, speak, muse. A primitive root; to murmur; by implication, to ponder.
Emptiness, a worthless thing, in vain. From ruwq; emptiness; figuratively, a worthless thing; adverbially, in vain.
2: Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To set or station oneself, take one's stand. A primitive root; to place; reflexively, to station, offer, continue.
To be weighty, judicious or commanding. A primitive root; probably to be heavy, i.e. honorable.
A primitive root; to set; intensively, to found; reflexively, to sit down together, i.e. Settle, consult.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Anointed. From mashach; anointed; usually a consecrated person; specifically, the Messiah.
3: To pull, draw, or tear away, apart, or off. A primitive root; to tear off.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
A band, bond. Also feminine mowcerah; or mocrah; from yacar; properly, chastisement, i.e. a halter; figuratively, restraint.
To throw, fling, cast. A primitive root; to throw out, down or away.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Cord, rope, cordage. Or rabowth; or tabothah; the same as aboth; something intwined, i.e. A string, wreath or foliage.
4: A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
To laugh. A primitive root; to laugh; by implication, to play.
Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
To mock, deride, stammer. A primitive root; to deride; by implication to speak unintelligibly.
5: At that time. A demonstrative adverb; at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
To disturb, terrify. A primitive root; to tremble inwardly, i.e. be alarmed or agitated; by implication to hasten anxiously.
(burning of) anger. Or charon; from charah; a burning of anger.
6: A primitive root; to pour out, especially a libation, or to cast; by analogy, to anoint a king.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A mountain in Jer., also a name for Jer. The same as tsiyuwn; Tsijon, a mountain of Jerusalem.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
7: Something prescribed or owed, a statute. From chaqaq; an enactment; hence, an appointment.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
A primitive root; to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage.
8: To ask, inquire. Or shael; a primitive root; to inquire; by implication, to request; by extension, to demand.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
From nachal; properly, something inherited, i.e. occupancy, or an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion.
From 'aphec; cessation, i.e. An end; often used adverb, no further; also the ankle, as being the extremity of the leg or foot.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A possession. Feminine passive participle from 'achaz; something seized, i.e. A possession.
9: A primitive root; properly, to spoil; figuratively, to make good for nothing, i.e. Bad.
Iron. Perhaps from the root of Birzowth; iron; by extension, an iron implement.
Rod, staff, club, scepter, tribe. From an unused root probably meaning to branch off; a scion, i.e. a stick Or a clan.
To dash to pieces, scatter. A primitive root; to dash to pieces, or scatter.
Probably identical with yatsar;); to mould into a form; especially as a potter; figuratively, to determine (i.e. Form a resolution).
An article, utensil, vessel. From kalah; something prepared, i.e. Any apparatus.
10: Now. From eth; at this time, whether adverb, conjunction or expletive.
To be, circumspect, intelligent. A primitive root; to be circumspect and hence, intelligent.
To discipline, chasten, admonish. A primitive root; to chastise, literally or figuratively; hence, to instruct.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
11: To work, serve. A primitive root; to work; by implication, to serve, till, enslave, etc.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A fear. Feminine of yare'; fear; morally, reverence.
To rejoice. Or guwl; a primitive root; properly, to spin round, i.e. Usually rejoice, or fear.
A trembling. Or radah; from ra'ad; a shudder.
12: To kiss, to equip with weapons. A primitive root; to kiss, literally or figuratively; also, to equip with weapons.
Son. Borrowed from bar; the heir.
Lest. From panah; properly, removal; used only adverb as conjunction, lest.
To be angry. A primitive root; to breathe hard, i.e. Be enraged.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
To kindle, consume, to be, brutish. A primitive root; to kindle, i.e. Consume; also to be(-come) brutish.
A little, fewness, a few. Or miat; from ma'at; a little or few.
Happiness, blessedness. From 'ashar; happiness; only in masculine plural construction as interjection, how happy!
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To seek refuge. A primitive root; to flee for protection; figuratively, to confide in.

1: A melody. From zamar; properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes.
Perhaps beloved one, a son of Jesse. Rarely; Daviyd; from the same as dowd; loving; David, the youngest son of Jesse.
To go through, flee. A primitive root; to bolt, i.e. Figuratively, to flee suddenly.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Or babshalowm; from 'ab and shalowm; father of peace; Abshalom, a son of David; also a later Israelite.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
A primitive root; properly, to cast together, i.e. Increase, especially in number; also to multiply by the myriad.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
2: Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
Salvation. Feminine passive participle of yasha'; something saved, i.e. deliverance; hence, aid, victory, prosperity.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
3: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A shield. Also feminine mginnah; from ganan; a shield; figuratively, a protector; also the scaly hide of the crocodile.
From ad with prepositional prefix; in up to or over against; generally at, beside, among, behind, for, etc.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
To be high or exalted, rise. A primitive root; to be high actively, to rise or raise.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
4: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
5: To lie down. A primitive root; to lie down.
To sleep. A primitive root; properly, to be slack or languid, i.e. sleep; also to grow old, stale or inveterate.
Arise, be awake, watch. A primitive root (compare yaqats); to awake.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To lean, lay, rest, support. A primitive root; to prop; reflexively, to lean upon or take hold of.
6: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
Multitude, myriad, ten thousand. From rabab; abundance, i.e. a myriad.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
7: To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To smite. A primitive root; to strike.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
Jaw, cheek. From an unused root meaning to be soft; the cheek; hence, the jaw-bone.
To break, break in pieces. A primitive root; to burst.
Tooth, ivory. From shanan; a tooth; specifically ivory; figuratively, a cliff.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
8: Salvation. Feminine passive participle of yasha'; something saved, i.e. deliverance; hence, aid, victory, prosperity.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A blessing. From barak; benediction; by implication prosperity.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.

1: Preeminent or enduring. A primitive root; properly, to glitter from afar, i.e. To be eminent; also, to be permanent.
A melody. From zamar; properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes.
Perhaps beloved one, a son of Jesse. Rarely; Daviyd; from the same as dowd; loving; David, the youngest son of Jesse.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Rightness, righteousness. From tsadaq; the right; also equity or prosperity.

Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
A primitive root; properly, to bend or stoop in kindness to an inferior; to favor, bestow; causatively to implore.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Prayer. From palal; intercession, supplication; by implication, a hymn.
2: As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
Insult, reproach, ignominy. From kalam; disgrace.
To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
Emptiness, a worthless thing, in vain. From ruwq; emptiness; figuratively, a worthless thing; adverbially, in vain.
To seek. A primitive root; to search out; by implication, to strive after.
A lie, falsehood, deceptive thing. From kazab; falsehood; literally or figuratively.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
3: A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To be separated or distinct. A primitive root; to distinguish.
Kind, pious. From chacad; properly, kind, i.e. pious.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
4: To be agitated, quiver, quake, be excited, perturbed. A primitive root; to quiver.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
A primitive root; properly, to miss; hence to sin; by inference, to forfeit, lack, expiate, repent, lead astray, condemn.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Place of lying, a couch, act of lying. From shakab; a bed; abstractly, sleep; by euphemism, carnal intercourse.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
A prim root; to be dumb; by implication, to be astonished, to stop; also to perish.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
5: To slaughter for sacrifice. A primitive root; to slaughter an animal.
A sacrifice. From zabach; properly, a slaughter, i.e. The flesh of an animal; by implication, a sacrifice.
Rightness, righteousness. From tsadaq; the right; also equity or prosperity.
To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
6: Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
7: To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
Joy, gladness, mirth. From sameach; blithesomeness or glee.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
Corn, grain (of cereals). From dagah; properly, increase, i.e. Grain.
Or tiyrosh; from yarash in the sense of expulsion; must or fresh grape-juice; by implication fermented wine.
A primitive root; properly, to cast together, i.e. Increase, especially in number; also to multiply by the myriad.
8: Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
To lie down. A primitive root; to lie down.
To sleep. A primitive root; properly, to be slack or languid, i.e. sleep; also to grow old, stale or inveterate.
Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.
Isolation, separation. From badad; separate; adverb, separately.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
Security. From batach; properly, a place of refuge; abstract, safety, both the fact and the feeling; often safely.

Perhaps flute (a musical instrument). Probably denominative from chaliyl; a flute.
A melody. From zamar; properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes.
Perhaps beloved one, a son of Jesse. Rarely; Daviyd; from the same as dowd; loving; David, the youngest son of Jesse.
A primitive root; probably to expand; but used only as a denominative from 'ozen; to broaden out the ear, i.e. to listen.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
A whisper, musing, murmuring. From an unused root akin to hagah; properly, a murmur, i.e. Complaint.
2: To incline (ears), attend. A primitive root; to prick up the ears, i.e. Hearken.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
A halloo. From shava'; a halloo.

The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
Morn- ing. From baqar; properly, dawn; generally, morning.
To set in a, row, arrange, put in order. A primitive root; to set in a row, i.e. Arrange, put in order.
A primitive root; properly, to lean forward, i.e. To peer into the distance; by implication, to observe, await.
4: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
Delighting in, having pleasure in. From chaphets; pleased with.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
A primitive root; properly, to turn aside from the road, i.e. Sojourn; also to shrink, fear; also to gather for hostility.
5: From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To set or station oneself, take one's stand. A primitive root; to place; reflexively, to station, offer, continue.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To hate. A primitive root; to hate.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, especially to practise.
From an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant; strictly nothingness; also trouble. Vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
6: To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
A lie, falsehood, deceptive thing. From kazab; falsehood; literally or figuratively.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To abhor. A primitive root; to loathe, i.e. detest.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
Deceit, treachery. From ramah in the sense of deceiving; fraud.
7: To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.
Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.

To bow down. A primitive root; to depress, i.e. Prostrate.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
A palace, temple. Probably from yakol; a large public building, such as a palace or temple.

The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
From anah; properly, heed, i.e. Purpose; used only adverbially, on account of, teleologically, in order that.
To be hostile. A primitive root; to be hostile.
To be smooth, straight, or right. A primitive root; to be straight or even; figuratively, to be right, pleasant, prosperous.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.

9: As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.

Neck, throat. Or garon; from garar; the throat.

A grave, sepulcher. Or qibrah; from qabar; a sepulchre.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.

Counsel, plan, principle, device. From ya'ats; a purpose.
To impel, thrust, banish. A primitive root; to push off; used in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively.
Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.

let them ever shout for joy.

Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.

The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Pure, clean. From barar; beloved; also pure, empty.
To be or become light. A primitive root; to be luminous.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
9: A fear. Feminine of yare'; fear; morally, reverence.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Clean, pure. Or tahor; from taher; pure.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
Perpetuity. From adah; properly, a terminus, i.e. duration, in the sense of advance or perpetuity.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Firmness, faithfulness, truth. Contracted from 'aman; stability; certainty, truth, trustworthiness.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
To be just or righteous. A primitive root; to be right.
10: To desire, take pleasure in. A primitive root; to delight in.
Gold. From an unused root meaning to shimmer; gold, figuratively, something gold-colored, as oil, a clear sky.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Refined, pure gold. From pazaz; pure; hence, gold itself.
Sweet, sweetness. Or mathuwq; from mathaq; sweet.
Honey. From an unused root meaning to be gummy; honey; by analogy, syrup.
(honey) comb. From tsuwph; comb of honey.
Flowing honey, honey from the comb. From nuwph in the sense of shaking to pieces; a dripping i.e. Of honey.
11: Slave, servant. From abad; a servant.
To gleam, to enlighten. A primitive root; to gleam; figuratively, to enlighten.
To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
From aqab in the sense of aqeb; a heel, i.e. the last of anything; also result, i.e. Compensation; and so on account of.
12: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
Error. From shagah; a moral mistake.
To be empty or clean. A primitive root; to be clean; by implication to be bare, i.e. Extirpated.
To hide, conceal. A primitive root; to hide, literally or figuratively.
13: To withhold, refrain. A primitive root; to restrain or Refrain; by implication, to refuse, spare, preserve; also to observe.
Slave, servant. From abad; a servant.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To rule, have dominion, reign. A primitive root; to rule.
At that time. A demonstrative adverb; at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore.
A primitive root; to complete, in a good or a bad sense, literal, or figurative, transitive or intransitive.
To be empty or clean. A primitive root; to be clean; by implication to be bare, i.e. Extirpated.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
14: Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
Resounding music, meditation, musing. Intensive from hagah; a murmuring sound, i.e. A musical notation; by implication, a machination.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Goodwill, favor, acceptance, will. Or ratson; from ratsah; delight.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
To redeem, act as kinsman. A primitive root, to redeem, i.e. To be the next of kin.

The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.

A primitive root; to be lofty, especially inaccessible; by implication, safe, strong; used literally and figuratively.
2: To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
A help, helper. From azar; aid.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
To support, sustain, stay. A primitive root; to suport.
A mountain in Jer., also a name for Jer. The same as tsiyuwn; Tsijon, a mountain of Jerusalem.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
From an unused root meaning to apportion, i.e. Bestow; a donation; euphemistically, tribute; specifically a sacrificial offering.
A primitive root; to be fat; transitively, to fatten; specifically to anoint; figuratively, to satisfy; denominatively to remove ashes.
Whole burnt offering. Or mowlah; feminine active participle of alah; a step or; usually a holocaust.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
4: To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Counsel, advice. From ya'ats; advice; by implication, plan; also prudence.
5: To give a ringing cry. A primitive root; properly, to creak, i.e. To shout.
Salvation. Feminine passive participle of yasha'; something saved, i.e. deliverance; hence, aid, victory, prosperity.
To flaunt, raise a, flag, to be conspicuous. A primitive root; to flaunt, i.e. Raise a flag; figuratively, to be conspicuous.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God

To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Request, petition. From sha'al; a request.
6: Now. From eth; at this time, whether adverb, conjunction or expletive.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.
Anointed. From mashach; anointed; usually a consecrated person; specifically, the Messiah.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
Deliverance, rescue, salvation, safety, welfare. Or yeshai; from yasha'; liberty, deliverance, prosperity.
Strength, might. Feminine passive participle from the same as gibbowr; force; by implication, valor, victory.
Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.
7: These. Prolonged from 'el; these or those.
From rakab; a vehicle; by implication, a team; by extension, cavalry; by analogy a rider, i.e. The upper millstone.
These. Prolonged from 'el; these or those.
A swallow, swift (type of bird). Or cuc; from an unused root meaning to skip; a horse; also a swallow.
Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.

8: They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
To bow down. A primitive root; to bend the knee; by implication, to sink, to prostrate.
To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.

To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
Or goral; from an unused root meaning to be rough; properly, a pebble, i.e. A lot; figuratively, a portion or destiny.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A garment, clothing, raiment. Or lbush; from labash; a garment; by implication A wife.
19: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To be or become far or distant. A primitive root; to widen, i.e. recede or remove.
My help. Feminine of 'eyal; power; by implication, protection.
A primitive root; to hurry; figuratively, to be eager with excitement or enjoyment.
Help, helper, assistance. Or mezrath; feminine of ezer; aid.
20: To strip, plunder, deliver oneself, be delivered, snatch away, deliver.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
Only, only one, solitary. From yachad; properly, united, i.e. Sole; by implication, beloved; also lonely; the life.
A dog. From an unused root means. To yelp, or else to attack; a dog; hence a male prostitute.
21: To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
A lion. Or earyeh; from 'arah; a lion.
From qaran; a horn; by implication, a flask, cornet; by resembl. An elephant's tooth, a corner, a peak, a ray; figuratively, power.
A wild ox. Or rieym; or reym; or rem; from ra'am; a wild bull.
22: A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).
Midst. From an unused root meaning to sever; a bisection, i.e. the centre.
Assembly, convocation, congregation. From qahal; assemblage.
23: Fearing, reverent. From yare'; fearing; morally, reverent.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A sowing, seed, offspring. From zara'; seed; figuratively, fruit, plant, sowing-time, posterity.
A son of Isaac, also his desc. From aqab; heel-catcher; Jaakob, the Israelitish patriarch.
Or kabed; a primitive root; to be heavy, i.e. In a bad sense or in a good sense (in the same two senses).
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A sowing, seed, offspring. From zara'; seed; figuratively, fruit, plant, sowing-time, posterity.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
A primitive root; properly, to turn aside from the road, i.e. Sojourn; also to shrink, fear; also to gather for hostility.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
24: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To despise. A primitive root; to disesteem.
To detest, make detestable. A primitive root; to be filthy, i.e. to loathe, pollute.
Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

To hide, conceal. A primitive root; to hide, literally or figuratively.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To cry out (for help). A primitive root; properly, to be free; but used only causatively and reflexively, to halloo.
25: Praise, song of praise. From halal; laudation; specifically a hymn.
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Assembly, convocation, congregation. From qahal; assemblage.
A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
A vow. Or neder; from nadar; a promise; also a thing promised.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
Fearing, reverent. From yare'; fearing; morally, reverent.
26: Poor, afflicted, humble, meek. Or Aanayv; from anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
To be sated, satisfied or surfeited. Or sabeay; a primitive root; to sate, i.e. Fill to satisfaction.
A primitive root; properly, to tread or frequent; usually to follow; by implication, to seek or ask; specifically to worship.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
To live, to revive. A primitive root; to live, whether literally or figuratively; causatively, to revive.
Perpetuity. From adah; properly, a terminus, i.e. duration, in the sense of advance or perpetuity.
27: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
From 'aphec; cessation, i.e. An end; often used adverb, no further; also the ankle, as being the extremity of the leg or foot.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

To bow down. A primitive root; to depress, i.e. Prostrate.

30: A sowing, seed, offspring. From zara'; seed; figuratively, fruit, plant, sowing-time, posterity.
To work, serve. A primitive root; to work; by implication, to serve, till, enslave, etc.
Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
Period, generation, dwelling. Or dor; from duwr; properly, a revolution of time, i.e. An age or generation; also a dwelling.
31: To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
A primitive root; to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.

Perhaps beloved one, a son of Jesse. Rarely; Daviyd; from the same as dowd; loving; David, the youngest son of Jesse.

Grass. From dasha; a sprout; by analogy, grass.
Habitation, house, pasture, pleasant place. From na'ah; a home; figuratively, a pasture.

Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
3: A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.

Death-like shadow, deep shadow. From tsel and maveth; shade of death, i.e. The grave.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.

From an unused root meaning to hold together; a cup, often figuratively, a lot; also some unclean bird, probably an owl.

Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.

Bone, substance, self. From atsam; a bone; by extension, the body; figuratively, the substance, i.e. Selfsame.
To waste away. A primitive root; probably to shrink, i.e. Fail.
11: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A primitive root; to cramp, literally or figuratively, transitive or intransitive.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A reproach. From charaph. contumely, disgrace, the pudenda.
Inhabitant, neighbor. From shakan; a resident; by extension, a fellow-citizen.
Muchness, force, abundance. From the same as 'uwd; properly, vehemence, i.e. vehemently; by implication, wholly, speedily, etc.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
A primitive root; properly, to wave to and fro; figuratively, to rove, flee, or to drive away.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
The outside, a street. Or chuts; from an unused root meaning to sever; properly, separate by a wall, i.e. Outside, outdoors.
12: To forget. Or shakeach; a primitive root; to mislay, i.e. To be oblivious of, from want of memory or attention.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
An article, utensil, vessel. From kalah; something prepared, i.e. Any apparatus.
13: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Whispering, defamation, evil report. From dabab; slander.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Fear, terror. Or maguwr; from guwr in the sense of fearing; a fright.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
A primitive root; to set; intensively, to found; reflexively, to sit down together, i.e. Settle, consult.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To consider, purpose, devise. A primitive root; to plan, usually in a bad sense.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
14: To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.

15: Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
To strip, plunder, deliver oneself, be delivered, snatch away, deliver.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
and from those who pursue me.
To pursue, chase, persecute. A primitive root; to run after (of time) gone by).
16: Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To be or become light. A primitive root; to be luminous.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Slave, servant. From abad; a servant.
To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.
Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.
17: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To be ashamed. A primitive root; properly, to pale, i.e. By implication to be ashamed; also to be disappointed or delayed.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To be ashamed. A primitive root; properly, to pale, i.e. By implication to be ashamed; also to be disappointed or delayed.
A prim root; to be dumb; by implication, to be astonished, to stop; also to perish.
Or shol; from sha'al; Hades or the world of the dead, including its accessories and inmates.
18: Deception, disappointment, falsehood. From shaqar; an untruth; by implication, a sham.
Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
To bind. A primitive root; to tie fast; hence to be tongue-tied.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
Forward, arrogant. From athaq in the sense of license; impudent.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Majesty, pride. From ga'ah; arrogance or majesty; by implication, ornament.
19: Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Good things, goods, goodness. From towb; good, in the widest sense, especially goodness, beauty, gladness, welfare.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
A primitive root; to hide; by implication, to hoard or reserve; figuratively to deny; specifically to protect, to lurk.
Fearing, reverent. From yare'; fearing; morally, reverent.
Do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, especially to practise.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
To seek refuge. A primitive root; to flee for protection; figuratively, to confide in.
20: To hide, conceal. A primitive root; to hide, literally or figuratively.
A covering, hiding place, secrecy. Or cithrah,; from cathar; a cover.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Perhaps conspiracy. From rakac; a snare.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
A primitive root; to hide; by implication, to hoard or reserve; figuratively to deny; specifically to protect, to lurk.
A thicket, booth. Fem of cok; a hut or lair.
Strife, dispute. Or rib; from riyb; a contest.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
21: To kneel, bless. A primitive root; to kneel; by implication to bless God, and man; also to curse.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A primitive root; properly, perhaps to separate, i.e. Distinguish; by implication, to be great, difficult, wonderful.
Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
Siege enclosure, siege, entrenchment. Or matsuwr; from tsuwr; something hemming in, i.e. a mound, a siege, distress; or a fastness.
22: To be in trepidation, hurry or alarm. A primitive root; properly, to start up suddenly, i.e. to hasten away, to fear.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To cut, cut off. A primitive root; to cut off.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
Surely. From kuwn; firmly; figuratively, surely; also But.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
Supplication for favor. Or tachanuwnah; from chanan; earnest prayer.
To cry out (for help). A primitive root; properly, to be free; but used only causatively and reflexively, to halloo.
23: To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Kind, pious. From chacad; properly, kind, i.e. pious.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To watch, guard, keep. A primitive root; to guard, in a good sense Or a bad one.
Faithfulness. From 'aman; established, i.e. trusty; also trustworthiness.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
From yathar; properly, an overhanging, i.e. an excess, superiority, remainder; also a small rope.
A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Majesty, pride. From ga'ah; arrogance or majesty; by implication, ornament.
24: A primitive root; to fasten upon; hence, to seize, be strong, obstinate; to bind, restrain, conquer.
To be stout, strong, bold, alert. A primitive root; to be alert, physically or mentally.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To wait, await. A primitive root; to wait; by implication, to be patient, hope.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.

17: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To forget. Or shakeach; a primitive root; to mislay, i.e. To be oblivious of, from want of memory or attention.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To do or deal falsely. A primitive root; to cheat, i.e. Be untrue.
A covenant. From barah (like bara'); a compact.
18: A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To move away, backslide. A primitive root; properly, to flinch, i.e. to go back, literally or figuratively.
The hind side, back part. Or achor; from Achiyra'; the hinder part; hence behind, backward; also the West.
Step, going. Or ashshur; from 'ashar in the sense of going; a step.
A primitive root; to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away; used in a great variety of application.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
A way, path. From 'arach; a well-trodden road; also a caravan.
19: To crush. A primitive root; to collapse.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
Serpent, dragon, sea monster. Or tanniym; intensive from the same as tan; a marine or land monster, i.e. Sea-serpent or jackal.
To cover. A primitive root; properly, to plump, i.e. Fill up hollows; by implication, to cover.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Death-like shadow, deep shadow. From tsel and maveth; shade of death, i.e. The grave.
20: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To forget. Or shakeach; a primitive root; to mislay, i.e. To be oblivious of, from want of memory or attention.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To spread out, spread. A primitive root; to break apart, disperse, etc.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
A primitive root; to turn aside; hence to be a foreigner, strange, profane; specifically to commit adultery.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
21: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
To search. A primitive root; properly, to penetrate; hence, to examine intimately.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
A hidden thing, secret. From alam; a secret.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
22: Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To kill, slay. A primitive root; to smite with deadly intent.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Small cattle, sheep and goats, flock. Or tsaown; from an unused root meaning to migrate; a collective name for a flock; also figuratively.
Thing slaughtered, slaughtered meat, slaughter. Feminine of tebach and meaning the same.
23: To rouse oneself, awake. A primitive root; to wake.
Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
To sleep. A primitive root; properly, to be slack or languid, i.e. sleep; also to grow old, stale or inveterate.
Arise, be awake, watch. A primitive root (compare yaqats); to awake.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
Reject, forsake, fail. A primitive root meaning to push aside, i.e. Reject, forsake, fail.
24: To hide, conceal. A primitive root; to hide, literally or figuratively.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To forget. Or shakeach; a primitive root; to mislay, i.e. To be oblivious of, from want of memory or attention.
Affliction, poverty. From anah; depression, i.e. Misery.
25: From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
To sink down. A primitive root; to sink, literally or figuratively.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
From an unused root probably meaning to be hollow; the belly, especially the womb; also the bosom or body of anything.
To cling, cleave, keep close. A primitive root; properly, to impinge, i.e. Cling or adhere; figuratively, to catch by pursuit.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
26: To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
Help, helper, assistance. Or mezrath; feminine of ezer; aid.
To ransom. A primitive root; to sever, i.e. Ransom; gener. To release, preserve.
From anah; properly, heed, i.e. Purpose; used only adverbially, on account of, teleologically, in order that.
Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.

1: Preeminent or enduring. A primitive root; properly, to glitter from afar, i.e. To be eminent; also, to be permanent.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Or showshan; or shoshan; and showshannah; from suws; a lily, as a flower of arch. Ornament; also a trumpet: lily, Shoshannim.
Instructive, a didactic poem. From sakal; instructive, i.e. A didactic poem.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
An Edomite name, also an Isr. name. From qarach; ice; Korach, the name of two Edomites and three Israelites.
Beloved. From the same as dowd; loved.
A song, singing. Or feminine shiyrah; from shiyr; a song; abstractly, singing.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To keep moving, stir. A primitive root; to gush.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
A deed, work. From asah; an action; generally, a transaction; abstractly, activity; by implication, a product or property.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
A stylus. From iyt in the sense of swooping, i.e. Side-long stroke; a stylus or marking stick.
Quick, prompt, ready, skilled. Or mahir; from mahar; quick; hence, skilful.
2: To be fair or beautiful. A primitive root; properly, to be bright, i.e. beautiful.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
Favor, grace. From chanan; graciousness, i.e. Subjective or objective.
A primitive root; properly, to pour out; by implication, to melt or cast as metal; by extension, to place firmly, to stiffen or grow hard.
Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To kneel, bless. A primitive root; to kneel; by implication to bless God, and man; also to curse.
3: To gird, gird on, gird oneself. A primitive root; to gird on.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
From an unused root meaning to be soft; the thigh; by euphem. The generative parts; figuratively, a shank, flank, side.
Strong, mighty. Or gibbor; intensive from the same as geber; powerful; by implication, warrior, tyrant.
Splendor, majesty, vigor. From an unused root; grandeur.
An ornament, honor, splendor. From hadar; magnificence, i.e. Ornament or splendor.
4: An ornament, honor, splendor. From hadar; magnificence, i.e. Ornament or splendor.
To mount and ride, ride. A primitive root; to ride; causatively, to place upon, to despatch.
To push forward. Or tsaleach; a primitive root; to push forward, in various senses.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
Firmness, faithfulness, truth. Contracted from 'aman; stability; certainty, truth, trustworthiness.
Gentleness, meekness. Feminine of anav; mildness; also oppressed.
Rightness, righteousness. From tsadaq; the right; also equity or prosperity.
Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.
Or yara; a primitive root; properly, to flow as water; transitively, to lay or throw; figuratively, to point out, to teach.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
5: From chatsats; properly, a piercer, i.e. An arrow; by implication, a wound; figuratively, thunder-bolt; the shaft of a spear.
To whet, sharpen. A primitive root; to point; intensively, to pierce; figuratively, to inculcate.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
6: Seat of honor, throne. Or kicceh; from kacah; properly, covered, i.e. A throne.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Perpetuity. From adah; properly, a terminus, i.e. duration, in the sense of advance or perpetuity.
Rod, staff, club, scepter, tribe. From an unused root probably meaning to branch off; a scion, i.e. a stick Or a clan.
Rod, staff, club, scepter, tribe. From an unused root probably meaning to branch off; a scion, i.e. a stick Or a clan.
Royalty, royal power, reign, kingdom. Or malkuth; or malkuyah; from malak; a rule; concretely, a dominion.
7: To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
Rightness, righteousness. From tsadaq; the right; also equity or prosperity.
To hate. A primitive root; to hate.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.

To smear, anoint. A primitive root; to rub with oil, i.e. To anoint; by implication, to consecrate; also to paint.
United, associate, companion. From chabar; an associate.
Fat, oil. From shaman; grease, especially liquid; figuratively, richness.
Exultation, rejoicing. Or sason; from suws; cheerfulness; specifically, welcome.
8: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A covering, clothing, treachery, pillage. From bagad; a covering, i.e. Clothing; also treachery or pillage.
Myrrh. Or mowr; from marar; myrrh.
The aloe (a tree). Or ahalowth; of foreign origin; aloe wood.
Cassia (a powdered bark). From qatsa'; cassia.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
A palace, temple. Probably from yakol; a large public building, such as a palace or temple.
Tooth, ivory. From shanan; a tooth; specifically ivory; figuratively, a cliff.
String (of a harp). From an unused root meaning to apportion; a part; hence, a musical chord.
Rejoice, be glad. A primitive root; probably to brighten up, i.e. be blithe or gleesome.
9: Precious, rare, splendid, weighty. From yaqar; valuable.
To take one's stand, stand. A prim root; to station, in various applications.
Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.
Gold. From katham; properly, something carved out, i.e. Ore; hence, gold.
Or pophiyr; and fowphir; of uncertain derivation; Ophir, the name of a son of Joktan, and of a gold region in the East.
10: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
A primitive root; to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away; used in a great variety of application.
An ear. From 'azan; broadness. I.e. the ear.
To forget. Or shakeach; a primitive root; to mislay, i.e. To be oblivious of, from want of memory or attention.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
11: To incline, desire. A primitive root; to wish for.
To bow down. A primitive root; to depress, i.e. Prostrate.
Lord. Or adon; from an unused root; sovereign, i.e. Controller.
12: A Phoenician city. Or Tsowr; the same as tsor; a rock; Tsor, a place in Palestine.
From an unused root meaning to apportion, i.e. Bestow; a donation; euphemistically, tribute; specifically a sacrificial offering.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Rich. From ashar; rich, whether literal or figurative.
A primitive root; properly, to be rubbed or worn; hence to be weak, sick, afflicted; or to grieve, make sick; also to stroke, entreat.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
13: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Abundance, riches. Irreg. Feminine passive participle of kabad; weightiness, i.e. Magnificence, wealth.
Toward the side or inside, within. From paniym with directive enclitic; faceward, i.e. Indoors.
A garment, clothing, raiment. Or lbush; from labash; a garment; by implication A wife.
Checkered or plaited work. From shabats; a brocade; by analogy, a setting of a gem.
Gold. From an unused root meaning to shimmer; gold, figuratively, something gold-colored, as oil, a clear sky.
14: Variegated stuff. From raqam; variegation of color; specifically, embroidery.
To conduct, bear along. A primitive root; properly, to flow; causatively, to bring.
Feminine passive participle of an unused root meaning to separate; a virgin; sometimes a bride; also a city or state.
Companion, attendant. Feminine of rea'; a female associate.
The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
15: To conduct, bear along. A primitive root; properly, to flow; causatively, to bring.
Joy, gladness, mirth. From sameach; blithesomeness or glee.
A revolution, joy. From giyl; a revolution; also joy.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
A palace, temple. Probably from yakol; a large public building, such as a palace or temple.

Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
Chieftain, chief, ruler, official, captain, prince. From sarar; a head person.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
17: Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Period, generation, dwelling. Or dor; from duwr; properly, a revolution of time, i.e. An age or generation; also a dwelling.
