Gatherer, the name of several Isr. From 'acaph; collector; Asaph, the name of three Israelites, and of the family of the first.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Place of sunrise, the east. From zarach; sunrise, i.e. The east.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Entrance, a coming in, entering. From bow'; an entrance; specifically sunset or the west; also towards.
From an unused root meaning to be brilliant; the sun; by implication, the east; figuratively, a ray, i.e. A notched battlement.
2: A mountain in Jer., also a name for Jer. The same as tsiyuwn; Tsijon, a mountain of Jerusalem.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To shine out or forth, to send out beams, cause to shine. A primitive root; to shine.
3: Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To cut in, engrave, plow, devise.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To storm, to shiver, fear. A primitive root; to storm; by implication, to shiver, i.e. Fear.
Muchness, force, abundance. From the same as 'uwd; properly, vehemence, i.e. vehemently; by implication, wholly, speedily, etc.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
4: To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
Height. From alah; properly, the top; specifically, the highest; also aloft, to Jehovah.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To judge. Or duwn; a primitive roy a straight course, i.e. Sail direct.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
5: To gather, remove. A primitive root; to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. Remove.
Kind, pious. From chacad; properly, kind, i.e. pious.
To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
A covenant. From barah (like bara'); a compact.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A sacrifice. From zabach; properly, a slaughter, i.e. The flesh of an animal; by implication, a sacrifice.
6: Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
Rightness, righteousness. From tsadaq; the right; also equity or prosperity.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
7: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
A primitive root; to duplicate or repeat; by implication, to protest, testify; intensively, to encompass, restore.
I. Sometimes; a primitive pro. I.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
8: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To decide, adjudge, prove. A primitive root; to be right; reciprocal, to argue; causatively, to decide, justify or convict.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A sacrifice. From zabach; properly, a slaughter, i.e. The flesh of an animal; by implication, a sacrifice.
Whole burnt offering. Or mowlah; feminine active participle of alah; a step or; usually a holocaust.
From an unused root meaning to stretch; properly, continuance; but used only constant; ellipt. The regular sacrifice.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
9: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
Young bull, steer. Or par; from parar; a bullock.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Male goat. Or sattud; from athad; prepared, i.e. Full grown; spoken only of he-goats, or leaders of the people.
An enclosure, fold. Or miklah; from kala'; a pen.
10: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
From an unused root probably meaning to thicken with verdure; a copse of bushes; hence, a forest; hence, honey in the comb.
A beast, animal, cattle. From an unused root; properly, a dumb beast; especially any large quadruped or animal.
A thousand. Prop, the same as 'eleph; hence a thousand.
Hill, mountain. From an unused root meaning to loom up; a mountain.
11: A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Flying creatures. From uwph; a bird, often collectively.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
From an unused root apparently meaning to be conspicuous; fulness of the breast; also a moving creature.
Field, land. Or saday; from an unused root meaning to spread out; a field.
12: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To be hungry. A primitive root; to hunger.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
From yabal; the earth; by extension, the globe; by implication, its inhabitants; specifically, a partic. Land, as Babylonia, Palestine.
Fullness, that which fills. Rarely mlowf; or mlow,; from male'; fulness.
13: To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.
To drink. A primitive root; to imbibe.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
Male goat. Or sattud; from athad; prepared, i.e. Full grown; spoken only of he-goats, or leaders of the people.
14: To slaughter for sacrifice. A primitive root; to slaughter an animal.
From yadah; properly, an extension of the hand, i.e. avowal, or adoration; specifically, a choir of worshippers.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
A vow. Or neder; from nadar; a promise; also a thing promised.
An elevation, lofty, as title, the Supreme. From alah; an elevation, i.e. Lofty; as title, the Supreme.
15: To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
A primitive root; to pull off; hence to strip, To depart; by implication, to deliver, equip; present, strengthen.
Or kabed; a primitive root; to be heavy, i.e. In a bad sense or in a good sense (in the same two senses).
16: Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Something prescribed or owed, a statute. From chaqaq; an enactment; hence, an appointment.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
A covenant. From barah (like bara'); a compact.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
17: To hate. A primitive root; to hate.
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
To throw, fling, cast. A primitive root; to throw out, down or away.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
18: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
A thief. From ganab; a stealer.
To be pleased with, accept favorably. A primitive root; to be pleased with; specifically, to satisfy a debt.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
Portion, tract, territory. From chalaq; properly, smoothness; also an allotment.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
To commit adultery. A primitive root; to commit adultery; figuratively, to apostatize.
19: To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
To bind, join. A primitive root; to link, i.e. Gird; figuratively, to serve, contrive.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
Deceit, treachery. From ramah in the sense of deceiving; fraud.
20: A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
A blemish, fault. From an unused root; a stumbling-block.
A mother. A primitive word; a mother; in a wide sense (like 'ab).
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
21: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
These. Prolonged from 'el; these or those.
To cut in, engrave, plow, devise.
To be like, resemble. A primitive root; to compare; by implication, to resemble, liken, consider.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
To decide, adjudge, prove. A primitive root; to be right; reciprocal, to argue; causatively, to decide, justify or convict.
To set in a, row, arrange, put in order. A primitive root; to set in a row, i.e. Arrange, put in order.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
22: To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
To forget. Or shakeach; a primitive root; to mislay, i.e. To be oblivious of, from want of memory or attention.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
Lest. From panah; properly, removal; used only adverb as conjunction, lest.
To tear, rend, pluck. A primitive root; to pluck off or pull to pieces; causatively to supply with food.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To strip, plunder, deliver oneself, be delivered, snatch away, deliver.
23: To slaughter for sacrifice. A primitive root; to slaughter an animal.
From yadah; properly, an extension of the hand, i.e. avowal, or adoration; specifically, a choir of worshippers.
Or kabed; a primitive root; to be heavy, i.e. In a bad sense or in a good sense (in the same two senses).
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Deliverance, rescue, salvation, safety, welfare. Or yeshai; from yasha'; liberty, deliverance, prosperity.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God

A melody. From zamar; properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes.
Perhaps beloved one, a son of Jesse. Rarely; Daviyd; from the same as dowd; loving; David, the youngest son of Jesse.
The name of a number of Isr. From nathan; given; Nathan, the name of five Israelites.
A spokesman, speaker, prophet. From naba'; a prophet or inspired man.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
From bath and sheba'; daughter of an oath; Bath-Sheba, the mother of Solomon.
From bath and sheba'; daughter of an oath; Bath-Sheba, the mother of Solomon.
A primitive root; properly, to bend or stoop in kindness to an inferior; to favor, bestow; causatively to implore.

Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.
Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.
Compassion. From racham; compassion; by extension, the womb; by implication, a maiden.
To stroke, rub, to erase, to smooth, grease, make fat, to touch, reach to.
2: To be or become much, many or great. A primitive root; to increase.
To wash. A primitive root; to trample; hence, to wash, whether literal or figurative.
Iniquity, guilt, punishment for iniquity. Or oavown; from avah; perversity, i.e. evil.
To be clean or pure. A primitive root; properly, to be bright; i.e. to be pure.
Or chattacth; from chata'; an offence, and its penalty, occasion, sacrifice, or expiation; also an offender.

To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.

To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.

A mother. A primitive word; a mother; in a wide sense (like 'ab).
To be hot, to conceive. A primitive root; probably to be hot; figuratively, to conceive.

Firmness, faithfulness, truth. Contracted from 'aman; stability; certainty, truth, trustworthiness.

Hyssop. Probably of foreign derivation; hyssop.

Exultation, rejoicing. Or sason; from suws; cheerfulness; specifically, welcome.
Joy, gladness, mirth. From sameach; blithesomeness or glee.

Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.

3: To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Deception, disappointment, falsehood. From shaqar; an untruth; by implication, a sham.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
Rightness, righteousness. From tsadaq; the right; also equity or prosperity.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
4: To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
A swallowing, devouring, a thing swallowed. From bala'; a gulp; figuratively, destruction.
Deceit, treachery. From ramah in the sense of deceiving; fraud.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
5: God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
To pull down, break down. A primitive root; to tear down.
To snatch up. A primitive root; to lay hold of; especially to pick up fire.
To pull or tear away. A primitive root; to tear away.
To deal with the roots. A primitive root; to root, i.e. Strike into the soil, or to pluck from it.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
6: To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
they will mock the evildoer, saying,
To laugh. A primitive root; to laugh; by implication, to play.
7: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
Man. From gabar; properly, a valiant man or warrior; generally, a person simply.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God

To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.
To be strong. A primitive root; to be stout.
Desire, chasm, destruction. From hava'; desire; also ruin.
8: Olive tree, olive. Probably from an unused root; an olive, the tree, the branch or the berry.
Luxuriant, fresh. From an unused root meaning to be green; verdant; by analogy, new; figuratively, prosperous.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Perpetuity. From adah; properly, a terminus, i.e. duration, in the sense of advance or perpetuity.
9: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
To bind together, collect, to expect. A primitive root; to bind together, i.e. Collect; to expect.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
Kind, pious. From chacad; properly, kind, i.e. pious.

Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
In psalm titles, probably the name of a tune. From chalah; sickness; Machalath, probably the title of a popular song.
Instructive, a didactic poem. From sakal; instructive, i.e. A didactic poem.

To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.

Perhaps to go to ruin. A primitive root; to decay, i.e. ruin.
To abhor. A primitive root; to loathe, i.e. detest.
Injustice, unrighteousness. Or lavel; and lavlah; or owlah; or.olah; from aval; evil.

To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.

Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.

As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.

From an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant; strictly nothingness; also trouble. Vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

5: There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
To dread, be in dread or in awe. A primitive root; to be startled; hence, to fear in general.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

Bone, substance, self. From atsam; a bone; by extension, the body; figuratively, the substance, i.e. Selfsame.

From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.

From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.

From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
Rejoice, be glad. A primitive root; probably to brighten up, i.e. be blithe or gleesome.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.

Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.

A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.

They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
A drawn sword. From pathach; something opened, i.e. A drawn sword.

A lot, what is given. From yahab; properly, what is given, i.e. A lot.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To comprehend, contain. A primitive root; properly, to keep in; hence, to measure; figuratively, to maintain.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
To totter, shake, slip. A primitive root; to waver; by implication, to slip, shake, fall.

To come or go down, descend.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.

Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Leader of a choir of the temple. Or Yduthuwn; or Ydiythuwn; probably from yadah; laudatory; Jeduthun, an Israelite.
A melody. From zamar; properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes.
Perhaps beloved one, a son of Jesse. Rarely; Daviyd; from the same as dowd; loving; David, the youngest son of Jesse.

From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.

Salvation. Feminine passive participle of yasha'; something saved, i.e. deliverance; hence, aid, victory, prosperity.

A wall. Or qir; or qiyrah; from quwr; a wall.
To push, thrust. Or dachach daw-khakh'; a primitive root; to push down.
A wall. From gadar; a circumvallation; by implication, an inclosure.

From nasa'; an elevation or leprous scab; figuratively, elation or cheerfulness; exaltation in rank or character.

Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
To kneel, bless. A primitive root; to kneel; by implication to bless God, and man; also to curse.
To be slight, swift or trifling. A primitive root; to be light, literally Or figuratively.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To pour out, pour. A primitive root; to spill forth; also to expend; intensively, to sprawl out.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.

Refuge, shelter. Or machceh; from chacah; a shelter.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
9: Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
Or Habel; from habal; emptiness or vanity; figuratively, something transitory and unsatisfactory; often used as an adverb.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).

They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.

Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.

10: Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
Oppression, extortion. From ashaq; injury, fraud, distress, unjust gain.

Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
Robbery. From gazal; robbery, or plunder.
From chuwl; probably a force, whether of men, means or other resources; an army, wealth, virtue, valor, strength.
To bear fruit. A primitive root; to germinate, i.e. to flourish; also, to utter.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
11: Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
This, which, who. For zeh; this or that.
Two (a card. number). Dual of sheniy; feminine shttayim; two; also twofold.
Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.

Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.

Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.

Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.

Or cheleb; from an unused root meaning to be fat; fat, whether literally or figuratively; hence, the richest or choice part.

The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.

To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Lower, lowest. From tachath; lowermost; as noun the depths.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.

Fox, perhaps jackal. Or shunal; from the same as sho'al; a jackal.

Suddenness, suddenly. Or pith.om; from petha'; instantly.

Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.

To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.

Injustice, unrighteousness. Or lavel; and lavlah; or owlah; or.olah; from aval; evil.

The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.

Iniquity, guilt, punishment for iniquity. Or oavown; from avah; perversity, i.e. evil.

Good things, goods, goodness. From towb; good, in the widest sense, especially goodness, beauty, gladness, welfare.

Deliverance, rescue, salvation, safety, welfare. Or yeshai; from yasha'; liberty, deliverance, prosperity.

End, boundary. And qitsvah; from qatsah; a limit.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Or rachoq; from rachaq; remote, literally or figuratively, of place or time; specifically, precious; often used adverbially.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
6: A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
Or kowach; from an unused root meaning to be firm; vigor, literally or figuratively; also a large lizard.
To gird, encompass, equip. A primitive root; to belt.
Strength, might. Feminine passive participle from the same as gibbowr; force; by implication, valor, victory.
7: A primitive root; properly, to address in a loud tone, i.e. loud; figuratively, to pacify.
A roar (of waters, etc.), din, crash, uproar. From sha'ah; uproar; by implication, destruction.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
A roar (of waters, etc.), din, crash, uproar. From sha'ah; uproar; by implication, destruction.
A heap, wave, billow. From galal; something rolled, i.e. A heap of stone or dung, by analogy, a spring of water.
A sound, murmur, roar, crowd, abundance. Or hamon; from hamah; a noise, tumult, crowd; also disquietude, wealth.
People. Or l owm; from an unused root meaning to gather; a community.
8: A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
An end. From qatsah; a termination; also a portion; adverbially after.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
A sign. Probably from 'uwth; a signal, as a flag, beacon, monument, omen, prodigy, evidence, etc.
A place or act of going forth, issue, export, source, spring.
Morn- ing. From baqar; properly, dawn; generally, morning.
To give a ringing cry. A primitive root; properly, to creak, i.e. To shout.
9: To attend to, visit, muster, appoint. A primitive root; to visit; by analogy, to oversee, muster, charge, care for, miss, deposit, etc.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Probably to be abundant. A primitive root; to run after or over, i.e. Overflow.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
To be or become rich. A primitive root; properly, to accumulate; chiefly to grow rich.
A channel, canal. From palag; a rill.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Full. From male'; full or filling; also fulness; adverbially, fully.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
Corn, grain (of cereals). From dagah; properly, increase, i.e. Grain.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
10: To be saturated, drink one's fill. A primitive root; to slake the thirst.
A furrow. From an unused root meaning to accumulate; a bank or terrace.
To go down, descend. A primitive root; to sink, i.e. Descend; causatively, to press or lead down.
A furrow, cutting. Or gdudah; from gadad; a furrow.
To melt. A primitive root; to melt, i.e. Literally, or figuratively.
Abundant showers. From rabab; a rain.
To kneel, bless. A primitive root; to kneel; by implication to bless God, and man; also to curse.
A sprout, growth. From tsamach; a sprout, literal or figurative.
11: To encircle, to crown. A primitive root; to encircle; especially to crown.
A year. (in plura or shanah; from shanah; a year.
An entrenchment, track. Or feminine mapgalah; from the same as agol; a track; also a rampart.
To trickle, drip. A primitive root; to drip.
Fatness, ashes of fat. From dashen; the fat; abstractly fatness, i.e. abundance; specifically the ashes of sacrifices.
12: Habitation, house, pasture, pleasant place. From na'ah; a home; figuratively, a pasture.
A pasture, a desert, speech. From dabar in the sense of driving; a pasture; by implication, a desert; also speech.
To trickle, drip. A primitive root; to drip.
A hill. Feminine from the same as Geba'; a hillock.
To gird, gird on, gird oneself. A primitive root; to gird on.
A revolution, joy. From giyl; a revolution; also joy.
13: From karar in the sense of plumpness; a ram, including a battering-ram; hence, a meadow; also a pad or camel's saddle.
Or labesh; a primitive root; properly, wrap around, i.e. to put on a garment or clothe, literally or figuratively.
Small cattle, sheep and goats, flock. Or tsaown; from an unused root meaning to migrate; a collective name for a flock; also figuratively.
A vale. From amaq; a vale.
To shroud, clothe, to languish. A primitive root; to shroud, i.e. Clothe; hence to languish.
Grain, corn. Or bar; from barar; grain of any kind; by extens. The open country.
To raise a shout, give a blast. A primitive root; to mar; figuratively, to split the ears, i.e. Shout.
Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
To sing. Or shuwr; a primitive root; to sing.

Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.

Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.

A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
Dry land, dry ground. From yabesh; dry ground.

A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.

Rejoice, be glad. A primitive root; probably to brighten up, i.e. be blithe or gleesome.

Strength, might. Feminine passive participle from the same as gibbowr; force; by implication, valor, victory.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.

Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
Stubborn or rebellious. A primitive root; to turn away, i.e. be refractory.

Pain. Sometimes makcowb; also makfobah; from ka'ab; anguish or affliction.

Period, generation, dwelling. Or dor; from duwr; properly, a revolution of time, i.e. An age or generation; also a dwelling.
Strength, might. Feminine passive participle from the same as gibbowr; force; by implication, valor, victory.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
19: Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Height. From ruwm; altitude, i.e. Concretely, abstractly (elation), or adverbially (aloft).
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
20: Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
To live, to revive. A primitive root; to live, whether literally or figuratively; causatively, to revive.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Deep, sea, abyss. Or thom; from huwm; an abyss, especially the deep.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
21: To be or become much, many or great. A primitive root; to increase.
Greatness. Or gdullah; or gduwllah; feminine of gadowl; greatness; mighty acts.
A primitive root; properly, to sigh, i.e. Breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. to pity, console or rue; or to avenge.
A primitive root; to revolve, surround, or border; used in various applications, literally and figuratively.
22: An article, utensil, vessel. From kalah; something prepared, i.e. Any apparatus.
A skin-bag for, liquids, a vase, a lyre. Or nebel; from nabel; a skin-bag for liquids; hence, a vase; also a lyre.
Firmness, faithfulness, truth. Contracted from 'aman; stability; certainty, truth, trustworthiness.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To make music (in praise of God).
A lyre. From a unused root meaning to twang; a harp.
Sacred, holy. Or qadosh; from qadash; sacred; God, an angel, a saint, a sanctuary.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
23: To make music (in praise of God).
Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
To give a ringing cry. A primitive root; properly, to creak, i.e. To shout.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To ransom. A primitive root; to sever, i.e. Ransom; gener. To release, preserve.
24: Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
To moan, growl, utter, speak, muse. A primitive root; to murmur; by implication, to ponder.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
To seek. A primitive root; to search out; by implication, to strive after.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
To be ashamed. A primitive root; properly, to pale, i.e. By implication to be ashamed; also to be disappointed or delayed.
A primitive root: to blush; figuratively, to be ashamed, disappointed; causatively, to shame, reproach.

1: David's son and successor to his throne. From shalowm; peaceful; Shelomah, David's successor.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
2: To judge. Or duwn; a primitive roy a straight course, i.e. Sail direct.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Rightness, righteousness. From tsadaq; the right; also equity or prosperity.
Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
3: Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
A hill. Feminine from the same as Geba'; a hillock.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
4: Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
In want, needy, poor. From 'abah, in the sense of want; destitute.
To crush. A primitive root; to crumble; transitively, to bruise.
To oppress, wrong, extort. A primitive root; to press upon, i.e. Oppress, defraud, violate, overflow.
5: To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
From an unused root meaning to be brilliant; the sun; by implication, the east; figuratively, a ray, i.e. A notched battlement.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Moon. From the same as yerach; the moon.
Period, generation, dwelling. Or dor; from duwr; properly, a revolution of time, i.e. An age or generation; also a dwelling.
Period, generation, dwelling. Or dor; from duwr; properly, a revolution of time, i.e. An age or generation; also a dwelling.
6: To come or go down, descend.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A shearing, mowing. From gazaz; a fleece; also mown grass.
Abundant showers. From rabab; a rain.
A drop, dripping. By reduplication from an unused root meaning to flow; a pouring rain.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
7: A primitive root; to break forth as a bud, i.e. Bloom; generally, to spread; specifically, to fly; figuratively, to flourish.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.
Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Moon. From the same as yerach; the moon.
From balah; properly, failure, i.e. Nothing or destruction; usually without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.
8: To tread down, subjugate, to crumble off. A primitive root; to tread down, i.e. Subjugate; specifically, to crumble off.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
From 'aphec; cessation, i.e. An end; often used adverb, no further; also the ankle, as being the extremity of the leg or foot.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
9: From the same as tsiyah; a desert-dweller, i.e. Nomad or wild beast.
To bow down. A primitive root; to bend the knee; by implication, to sink, to prostrate.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
To lick. A primitive root; to lick.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
10: A son of Javan, his desc. and their land, also a port on the Mediterranean, also a Benjamite, also a Pers. noble.
Coast, region. From 'avah; properly, a habitable spot; dry land, a coast, an island.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
From an unused root meaning to apportion, i.e. Bestow; a donation; euphemistically, tribute; specifically a sacrificial offering.
Of foreign origin; Sheba, the name of three early progenitors of tribes and of an Ethiopian district.
Son of Cush, also his desc. and their land. Of foreign origin; Seba, a son of Cush, and the country settled by him.
To come near, approach. A primitive root; to approach for whatever purpose.
A gift. For sakar; a gratuity.
11: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To bow down. A primitive root; to depress, i.e. Prostrate.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
To work, serve. A primitive root; to work; by implication, to serve, till, enslave, etc.
12: To strip, plunder, deliver oneself, be delivered, snatch away, deliver.
In want, needy, poor. From 'abah, in the sense of want; destitute.
To cry out (for help). A primitive root; properly, to be free; but used only causatively and reflexively, to halloo.
Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To help, succor. A primitive root; to surround, i.e. Protect or aid.
13: To pity, look upon with compassion. A primitive root; properly, to cover, i.e. to compassionate.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Dangling, weak, thin. From dalal; properly, dangling, i.e. weak or thin.
In want, needy, poor. From 'abah, in the sense of want; destitute.

From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
In want, needy, poor. From 'abah, in the sense of want; destitute.
14: To redeem, act as kinsman. A primitive root, to redeem, i.e. To be the next of kin.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Injury, oppression. Or towk; from the same base as tavek; oppression.
Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
To be precious, prized or appraised. A primitive root; properly, apparently, to be heavy, i.e. valuable; causatively, to make rare.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
15: To live, to revive. A primitive root; to live, whether literally or figuratively; causatively, to revive.
Gold. From an unused root meaning to shimmer; gold, figuratively, something gold-colored, as oil, a clear sky.
Of foreign origin; Sheba, the name of three early progenitors of tribes and of an Ethiopian district.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
From an unused root meaning to stretch; properly, continuance; but used only constant; ellipt. The regular sacrifice.
To intervene, interpose. A primitive root; to judge; by extension, to intercede, pray.
From ad with prepositional prefix; in up to or over against; generally at, beside, among, behind, for, etc.
To kneel, bless. A primitive root; to kneel; by implication to bless God, and man; also to curse.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
16: To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Probably abundance, plenty. From pacac; expansion, i.e. Abundance.
Grain, corn. Or bar; from barar; grain of any kind; by extens. The open country.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To quake, shake. A primitive root; to undulate, partic. Through fear; specifically, to spring.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
To twinkle, glance, to blossom. A primitive root; to twinkle, i.e. Glance; by analogy, to blossom.
A wooded mountain range on the N. border of Isr. From lbab; white mountain; Lebanon, a mountain range in Palestine.
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
Herb, herbage. From an unused root meaning to glisten; grass.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
17: A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
To propagate, increase. A primitive root; to resprout, i.e. Propagate by shoots; figuratively, to be perpetual.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
From an unused root meaning to be brilliant; the sun; by implication, the east; figuratively, a ray, i.e. A notched battlement.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
To kneel, bless. A primitive root; to kneel; by implication to bless God, and man; also to curse.
To go straight, go on, advance. Or rasher; a primitive root; to be straight; figuratively, to go forward, be honest, proper.
18: To kneel, bless. A primitive root; to kneel; by implication to bless God, and man; also to curse.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.

From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
