19: To kneel, bless. A primitive root; to kneel; by implication to bless God, and man; also to curse.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Verily, truly. From 'aman; sure; abstract, faithfulness; adverb, truly.
Verily, truly. From 'aman; sure; abstract, faithfulness; adverb, truly.
20: To be complete, at an end, finished, accomplished, or spent. A primitive root; to end, whether intransitive or transitived.
Prayer. From palal; intercession, supplication; by implication, a hymn.
Perhaps beloved one, a son of Jesse. Rarely; Daviyd; from the same as dowd; loving; David, the youngest son of Jesse.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Father of David. By Aramaic Iyshay; from the same as yesh; extant; Jishai, David's father.

1: A melody. From zamar; properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes.
Gatherer, the name of several Isr. From 'acaph; collector; Asaph, the name of three Israelites, and of the family of the first.
Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
Pure, clean. From barar; beloved; also pure, empty.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
2: Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
A little, fewness, a few. Or miat; from ma'at; a little or few.
A primitive root; to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away; used in a great variety of application.
Step, going. Or ashshur; from 'ashar in the sense of going; a step.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To pour out, pour. A primitive root; to spill forth; also to expend; intensively, to sprawl out.
3: To be jealous or zealous. A primitive root; to be zealous, i.e. jealous or envious.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
4: As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
Bond, fetter, pang. Of uncertain derivation; a fetter; figuratively, a pain.
Death. From muwth; death; concretely, the dead, their place or state; figuratively, pestilence, ruin.
The body, powerful. From an unused root meaning to twist, i.e. be strong; the body; also powerful.
Fat. From bara'; fatted or plump.
5: As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
Trouble, labor, toil. From amal; toil, i.e. Wearing effort; hence, worry, wheth. Of body or mind.
Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; properly, to touch, i.e. Lay the hand upon; by implication, to reach; violently, to strike.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
6: So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Majesty, pride. From ga'ah; arrogance or majesty; by implication, ornament.
A garment. From shiyth; a dress.
Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
To shroud, clothe, to languish. A primitive root; to shroud, i.e. Clothe; hence to languish.
7: Or cheleb; from an unused root meaning to be fat; fat, whether literally or figuratively; hence, the richest or choice part.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
A showpiece, figure, imagination. From the same as Sekuw; a figure; figuratively, imagination.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
8: To mock, deride. A primitive root; to jeer, i.e. Blaspheme.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Height. From ruwm; altitude, i.e. Concretely, abstractly (elation), or adverbially (aloft).
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
Oppression, extortion. From ashaq; injury, fraud, distress, unjust gain.
9: To set, appoint. A primitive root; to place, i.e. Array; reflex. To lie.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
10: So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
To drain, drain out. A primitive root; to suck out; by implication, to drain, to squeeze out.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Full. From male'; full or filling; also fulness; adverbially, fully.
11: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
How?. Also eykah; and teykakah; prolonged from 'ay; how? Or how!; also where.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
An elevation, lofty, as title, the Supreme. From alah; an elevation, i.e. Lofty; as title, the Supreme.
12: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
These. Prolonged from 'el; these or those.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Quiet, at ease. Or shaleyv; feminine shlevah; from shalah; tranquil; careless; abstractly, security.
To grow, increase. A primitive root; to enlarge.
From chuwl; probably a force, whether of men, means or other resources; an army, wealth, virtue, valor, strength.
13: Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
Emptiness, a worthless thing, in vain. From ruwq; emptiness; figuratively, a worthless thing; adverbially, in vain.
I have kept my heart pure;
To be clear, clean or pure. A primitive root; to be translucent; figuratively, to be innocent.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
Innocency. Or niqqayon; from naqah; clearness.
To wash, wash off or away, bathe. A primitive root; to lave.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
14: To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A primitive root; properly, to touch, i.e. Lay the hand upon; by implication, to reach; violently, to strike.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
And towkachath; from yakach; chastisement; figuratively correction, refutation, proof.
Morn- ing. From baqar; properly, dawn; generally, morning.
15: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
To act or deal treacherously. A primitive root; to cover; figuratively, to act covertly; by implication, to pillage.
Period, generation, dwelling. Or dor; from duwr; properly, a revolution of time, i.e. An age or generation; also a dwelling.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
16: A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
Trouble, labor, toil. From amal; toil, i.e. Wearing effort; hence, worry, wheth. Of body or mind.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
17: As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
A sacred place, sanctuary. Or miqqdash; from qadash; a consecrated thing or place, especially, a palace, sanctuary or asylum.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
The after-part, end. From 'achar; the last or end, hence, the future; also posterity.
18: Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
Smoothness, flattery, an allotment. Feminine of cheleq; properly, smoothness; figuratively, flattery; also an allotment.
To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
Perhaps deceptions. Or mashshu ah; for mshow'ah; ruin.
19: How?. Also eykah; and teykakah; prolonged from 'ay; how? Or how!; also where.
A moment. From raga'. A wink, i.e. A very short space of time.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Waste, horror. From shamem; ruin; by implication, consternation.
A primitive root; to complete, in a good or a bad sense, literal, or figurative, transitive or intransitive.
To come to an end, cease. A primitive root; to snatch away, i.e. Terminate.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Terror, dreadful event, calamity, destruction. From balahh; alarm; hence, destruction.
20: Arise, be awake, watch. A primitive root (compare yaqats); to awake.
A dream. Or chalom; from chalam; a dream.
Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
To rouse oneself, awake. A primitive root; to wake.
To despise. A primitive root; to disesteem.
From an unused root meaning to shade; a phantom, i.e. illusion, resemblance; hence, a representative figure, especially an idol.
21: Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
To be pungent, in taste, in color. A primitive root; to be pungent; i.e. In taste, in color.
To whet, sharpen. A primitive root; to point; intensively, to pierce; figuratively, to inculcate.
A kidney. Feminine of kliy; a kidney; figuratively, the mind.
22: Brutishness. From ba'ar; properly, foot; i.e. Of cattle brutishness; stupid.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A beast, animal, cattle. From an unused root; properly, a dumb beast; especially any large quadruped or animal.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
23: From an unused root meaning to stretch; properly, continuance; but used only constant; ellipt. The regular sacrifice.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
To grasp, take hold, take possession. A primitive root; to seize.
Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.
24: To lead, guide. A primitive root; to guide; by implication, to transport.
Counsel, advice. From ya'ats; advice; by implication, plan; also prudence.
The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
25: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A primitive root; properly, to incline to; by implication to bend; figuratively, to be pleased with, desire.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
26: Flesh. From sha'ar; flesh, as living or for food; generally food of any kind; figuratively, kindred by blood.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
To be complete, at an end, finished, accomplished, or spent. A primitive root; to end, whether intransitive or transitived.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
Portion, tract, territory. From chalaq; properly, smoothness; also an allotment.
27: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
To put an end to, exterminate. A primitive root; to extirpate.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To commit fornication, be a harlot. A primitive root; to commit adultery; figuratively, to commit idolatry.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
28: Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
Refuge, shelter. Or machceh; from chacah; a shelter.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
From the same as mal'ak; properly, deputyship, i.e. Ministry; generally, employment or work; also property.

1: Instructive, a didactic poem. From sakal; instructive, i.e. A didactic poem.
Gatherer, the name of several Isr. From 'acaph; collector; Asaph, the name of three Israelites, and of the family of the first.
Reject, forsake, fail. A primitive root meaning to push aside, i.e. Reject, forsake, fail.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
To smoke, be angry. A primitive root; to smoke, whether literal or figurative.
Small cattle, sheep and goats, flock. Or tsaown; from an unused root meaning to migrate; a collective name for a flock; also figuratively.
A pasturing, shepherding, pasturage. From ra'ah in the sense of feeding; pasturage; concretely, a flock.
2: Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
Congregation. Feminine of ed in the original sense of fixture; a stated assemblage.
A primitive root; to erect, i.e. Create; by extension, to procure, especially by purchase; by implication to own.
The front, of place, time. Or qedmah; from qadam; the front, of place or time; often used adverbially.
To redeem, act as kinsman. A primitive root, to redeem, i.e. To be the next of kin.
Rod, staff, club, scepter, tribe. From an unused root probably meaning to branch off; a scion, i.e. a stick Or a clan.
From nachal; properly, something inherited, i.e. occupancy, or an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
A mountain in Jer., also a name for Jer. The same as tsiyuwn; Tsijon, a mountain of Jerusalem.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
A primitive root (by transmission) to shakab through the idea of lodging; compare cikluwth, shakam); to reside or permanently stay.
3: To be high or exalted, rise. A primitive root; to be high actively, to rise or raise.
A beat, foot, anvil, occurrence. Or pa;amah; from pa'am; a stroke, literally or figuratively.
Perhaps deceptions. Or mashshu ah; for mshow'ah; ruin.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
A primitive root; properly, to spoil; figuratively, to make good for nothing, i.e. Bad.
4: A primitive root; to cramp, literally or figuratively, transitive or intransitive.
To roar. A primitive root; to rumble or moan.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
A sign. Probably from 'uwth; a signal, as a flag, beacon, monument, omen, prodigy, evidence, etc.
A sign. Probably from 'uwth; a signal, as a flag, beacon, monument, omen, prodigy, evidence, etc.
5: A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
An axe. Perhaps from qadam in the sense of striking upon; an axe.
A thicket. From cabak, a copse.
Tree, trees, wood. From atsah; a tree; hence, wood.
From alah; properly, the upper part, used only adverbially with prefix upward, above, overhead, from the top, etc.
6: Now. From eth; at this time, whether adverb, conjunction or expletive.
To smite, hammer, strike down. A primitive root; to strike down; by implication, to hammer, stamp, conquer, disband.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
An axe. From kashal; properly, a feller, i.e. An axe.
An axe. From an unused root meaning to clap or strike with noise; a club or sledge-hammer.
7: To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
A sacred place, sanctuary. Or miqqdash; from qadash; a consecrated thing or place, especially, a palace, sanctuary or asylum.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A primitive root; properly, to bore, i.e. to wound, to dissolve; figuratively, to profane, to break, to begin; denom. to play.
Dwelling place, tabernacle. From shakan; a residence; specifically, the Tabernacle.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
8: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To oppress, maltreat, perhaps suppress. A primitive root; to rage or be violent: by implication, to suppress, to maltreat.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
To burn. A primitive root; to be on fire.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
9: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A sign. Probably from 'uwth; a signal, as a flag, beacon, monument, omen, prodigy, evidence, etc.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
A spokesman, speaker, prophet. From naba'; a prophet or inspired man.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
10: As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
When?. From an unused root meaning to extend; properly, extent; but used only adverbially, when.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
To pull off, to expose, to betroth, to carp at, defame, to spend the winter.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
To spurn, treat with contempt. A primitive root; to scorn; or by interchange for nuwts, to bloom.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
11: A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
Something prescribed or owed, a statute. From chaqaq; an enactment; hence, an appointment.
To be complete, at an end, finished, accomplished, or spent. A primitive root; to end, whether intransitive or transitived.
12: Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
The front, of place, time. Or qedmah; from qadam; the front, of place or time; often used adverbially.
Do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, especially to practise.
Salvation. Feminine passive participle of yasha'; something saved, i.e. deliverance; hence, aid, victory, prosperity.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
13: To break up, to violate, frustrate. A primitive root; to break up
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.
To break, break in pieces. A primitive root; to burst.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
Serpent, dragon, sea monster. Or tanniym; intensive from the same as tan; a marine or land monster, i.e. Sea-serpent or jackal.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
14: To crush. A primitive root; to crack in pieces, literally or figuratively.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
From lavah; a wreathed animal, i.e. A serpent; figuratively, the constellation of the dragon; also as a symbol of Bab.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
From the same as tsiyah; a desert-dweller, i.e. Nomad or wild beast.
15: To cleave, break open or through. A primitive root; to cleave; generally, to rend, break, rip or open.
A spring. Or ma ynow; or ma yanah; from ayin; a fountain, figuratively, a source.
Or nachlah; or nachalah; from nachal in its original sense; a stream, especially a winter torrent; a valley; also a shaft.
To be dry, dried up, or withered. A primitive root; to be ashamed, confused or disappointed; also to dry up or wither.
Perennial, ever-flowing, permanence. Or ethan; from an unused root; permanence; hence permanent; specifically a chieftain.
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
16: Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
From an unused root meaning to be brilliant; the sun; by implication, the east; figuratively, a ray, i.e. A notched battlement.
17: To take one's stand, stand. A prim root; to station, in various applications.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Border, boundary. Or gbulah; feminine of gbuwl; a boundary, region.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Probably identical with yatsar;); to mould into a form; especially as a potter; figuratively, to determine (i.e. Form a resolution).
To awake. From quwts; harvest, whether the product or the season.
Harvest time, autumn. From charaph. properly, the crop gathered, i.e. the autumn season; figuratively, ripeness of age.
18: Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
To pull off, to expose, to betroth, to carp at, defame, to spend the winter.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Foolish, senseless. From nabel; stupid; wicked.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To spurn, treat with contempt. A primitive root; to scorn; or by interchange for nuwts, to bloom.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
19: Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Turtledove. Or tor; probably the same as towr; a ring-dove, often as a term of endearment.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To forget. Or shakeach; a primitive root; to mislay, i.e. To be oblivious of, from want of memory or attention.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
20: To look. A primitive root; to scan, i.e. Look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care.
A covenant. From barah (like bara'); a compact.
Or navah; from navah; at home; hence lovely; also a home, of God, men, flocks, or wild animals.
Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
Dark place. From chashak; darkness; concretely, a dark place.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
21: Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
Crushed, oppressed. From an unused root; crushed, i.e. Injured.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
To be humiliated. A primitive root; properly, to wound; but only figuratively, to taunt or insult.
Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
In want, needy, poor. From 'abah, in the sense of want; destitute.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
22: To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Or ruwb; a primitive root; properly, to toss, i.e. Grapple; mostly figuratively, to wrangle, i.e. Hold a controversy; to defend.
Strife, dispute. Or rib; from riyb; a contest.
Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Foolish, senseless. From nabel; stupid; wicked.
A reproach. From charaph. contumely, disgrace, the pudenda.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
23: Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To forget. Or shakeach; a primitive root; to mislay, i.e. To be oblivious of, from want of memory or attention.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
A primitive root; to cramp, literally or figuratively, transitive or intransitive.
A roar (of waters, etc.), din, crash, uproar. From sha'ah; uproar; by implication, destruction.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
From an unused root meaning to stretch; properly, continuance; but used only constant; ellipt. The regular sacrifice.

1: Preeminent or enduring. A primitive root; properly, to glitter from afar, i.e. To be eminent; also, to be permanent.
From 'al and shachath; Thou must not destroy; probably the opening words to a popular song.
To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.”
From 'al and shachath; Thou must not destroy; probably the opening words to a popular song.
A melody. From zamar; properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes.
Gatherer, the name of several Isr. From 'acaph; collector; Asaph, the name of three Israelites, and of the family of the first.
A song, singing. Or feminine shiyrah; from shiyr; a song; abstractly, singing.

A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
Near. Or qarob; from qarab; near.

Evenness, uprightness, equity. From yashar; evenness, i.e. prosperity or concord; also straightness, i.e. rectitude.
3: Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.

West. Or maearabah; from arab, in the sense of shading; the west.

Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
2: A thicket, covert, lair. From cakak; a hut; also a lair.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Peaceful, an early name of Jer. The same as shalem; peaceful; Shalem, an early name of Jerusalem.
Habitation. Or msonah; feminine of ma'own, and meaning the same.
A mountain in Jer., also a name for Jer. The same as tsiyuwn; Tsijon, a mountain of Jerusalem.
3: There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
To break, break in pieces. A primitive root; to burst.
Flame. From saraph; a live coal; by analogy lightning; figuratively, an arrow,; specifically, fever.
A bow. From qashah in the original sense of bending: a bow, for shooting or the iris.
A shield. Also feminine mginnah; from ganan; a shield; figuratively, a protector; also the scaly hide of the crocodile.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
A battle, war. From lacham; a battle; generally, war.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
4: To be or become light. A primitive root; to be luminous.
Majestic. From 'adar; wide or large; figuratively, powerful.
Hill, mountain. From an unused root meaning to loom up; a mountain.
Prey, food, a leaf. From taraph; something torn, i.e. A fragment, e.g. A fresh leaf, prey, food.
5: A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.

To be drowsy, slumber. A primitive root; to slumber.
Sleep. Or shena; from yashen; sleep.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
From chuwl; probably a force, whether of men, means or other resources; an army, wealth, virtue, valor, strength.
6: A rebuke. From ga'ar; a chiding.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God

A swallow, swift (type of bird). Or cuc; from an unused root meaning to skip; a horse; also a swallow.
From rakab; a vehicle; by implication, a team; by extension, cavalry; by analogy a rider, i.e. The upper millstone.
To be in or fall into heavy sleep. A primitive root; to stun, i.e. Stupefy.

To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.

8: Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Judgment. From diyn; judgement; by impl. Also strife.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.

Or malilah; from alal in the sense of effecting; an exploit, or a performance; by implication, an opportunity.
A primitive root; properly, perhaps to separate, i.e. Distinguish; by implication, to be great, difficult, wonderful.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
12: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
A wonder. From pala'; a miracle.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A son of Ham, also his desc. and their country in N.W. Africa. Dual of matsowr; Mitsrajim, i.e. Upper and Lower Egypt.
Field, land. Or saday; from an unused root meaning to spread out; a field.
A place in Eg. Of Egyptian derivation; Tsoan, a place in Egypt.
13: To cleave, break open or through. A primitive root; to cleave; generally, to rend, break, rip or open.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
To take one's stand, stand. A prim root; to station, in various applications.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
A mound, wave. From nuwd in the sense of piling up; a mound, i.e. Wave.
14: To lead, guide. A primitive root; to guide; by implication, to transport.
A cloud mass, cloud. From anan; a cloud, i.e. The nimbus or thunder-cloud.
Daytime, by day. From yowm; daily.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.
15: To cleave, break open or through. A primitive root; to cleave; generally, to rend, break, rip or open.
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
A pasture, a desert, speech. From dabar in the sense of driving; a pasture; by implication, a desert; also speech.
Cause to drink water, give to drink. A primitive root; to quaff, i.e. to irrigate or furnish a potion to.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Deep, sea, abyss. Or thom; from huwm; an abyss, especially the deep.
16: A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
To flow, trickle, drop, distill. A primitive root; to drip, or shed by trickling.
A crag, cliff. From an unused root meaning to be lofty; a craggy rock, literally or figuratively.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To come or go down, descend.
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
17: To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
A primitive root; properly, to miss; hence to sin; by inference, to forfeit, lack, expiate, repent, lead astray, condemn.
To be contentious or rebellious. A primitive root; to be bitter; to rebel.
Dryness, drought. From an unused root meaning to parch; aridity; concretely, a desert.
An elevation, lofty, as title, the Supreme. From alah; an elevation, i.e. Lofty; as title, the Supreme.
18: Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
To test, try. A primitive root; to test; by implication, to attempt.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
To ask, inquire. Or shael; a primitive root; to inquire; by implication, to request; by extension, to demand.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
19: To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To be able, have power. Or yakowl; a primitive root; to be able, literally or morally.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
To set in a, row, arrange, put in order. A primitive root; to set in a row, i.e. Arrange, put in order.
A table. From shalach; a table; by implication, a meal.
A pasture, a desert, speech. From dabar in the sense of driving; a pasture; by implication, a desert; also speech.
20: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
To smite. A primitive root; to strike.
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To flow, gush. A primitive root; to flow freely, i.e. to have a flux; figuratively, to waste away; also to overflow.
Or nachlah; or nachalah; from nachal in its original sense; a stream, especially a winter torrent; a valley; also a shaft.
To overflow, rinse or wash off. A primitive root; to gush; by implication, to inundate, cleanse; by analogy, to gallop, conquer.
To be able, have power. Or yakowl; a primitive root; to be able, literally or morally.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Flesh. From sha'ar; flesh, as living or for food; generally food of any kind; figuratively, kindred by blood.
21: So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
To catch fire. A primitive root; to catch fire.
A son of Isaac, also his desc. From aqab; heel-catcher; Jaakob, the Israelitish patriarch.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
22: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
Salvation. Feminine passive participle of yasha'; something saved, i.e. deliverance; hence, aid, victory, prosperity.
23: To lay charge (upon), give charge (to), command, order. A primitive root; to constitute, enjoin.
Dust, cloud. From shachaq; a powder: by analogy, a thin vapor; by extension, the firmament.
From alah; properly, the upper part, used only adverbially with prefix upward, above, overhead, from the top, etc.
To open wide, to loosen, begin, plough, carve. A primitive root; to open wide; specifically, to loosen, begin, plough, carve.
A door. From dalah; something swinging, i.e. The valve of a door.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
24: To rain. A primitive root; to rain.
Manna (a kind of bread). From mah; literally, a whatness, i.e. Manna.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Corn, grain (of cereals). From dagah; properly, increase, i.e. Grain.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
25: Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
Provision, food. Or tsedah; feminine of tsayid; food.
Satiety, abundance. From saba'; satisfaction (figuratively) joy).
26: To pull out or up, set out, journey. A primitive root; properly, to pull up, especially the tent-pins, i.e. Start on a journey.
East, east wind. Or qadim; from qadam; the fore or front part; hence the East.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
A primitive root; to drive forth, i.e. Lead, carry away; reflexively, to proceed; also, to sigh.
South, south wind. Or teman; denominative from yamiyn; the south.
Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.
27: To rain. A primitive root; to rain.
Flesh. From sha'ar; flesh, as living or for food; generally food of any kind; figuratively, kindred by blood.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
Wing, extremity. From kanaph; an edge or extremity; specifically a wing, a flap, a quarter, a pinnacle.
Flying creatures. From uwph; a bird, often collectively.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
28: To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
An encampment, camp. From chanah; an encampment; hence, an army, whether literal or figurative.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
Dwelling place, tabernacle. From shakan; a residence; specifically, the Tabernacle.
29: To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Muchness, force, abundance. From the same as 'uwd; properly, vehemence, i.e. vehemently; by implication, wholly, speedily, etc.
To be sated, satisfied or surfeited. Or sabeay; a primitive root; to sate, i.e. Fill to satisfaction.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
A desire. From 'avah; a longing; by implication, a delight.
30: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to turn aside; hence to be a foreigner, strange, profane; specifically to commit adultery.
A desire. From 'avah; a longing; by implication, a delight.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.

To kill, slay. A primitive root; to smite with deadly intent.
From shaman; fat, i.e. fatness; but usually a rich dish, a fertile field, a robust man.
To bow down. A primitive root; to bend the knee; by implication, to sink, to prostrate.
A young man. Or bachur; participle passive of bachar; properly, selected, i.e. A youth.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
32: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Or Habel; from habal; emptiness or vanity; figuratively, something transitory and unsatisfactory; often used as an adverb.
A year. (in plura or shanah; from shanah; a year.
Dismay, sudden terror or ruin. From bahal; panic, destruction.
34: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To kill, slay. A primitive root; to smite with deadly intent.
A primitive root; properly, to tread or frequent; usually to follow; by implication, to seek or ask; specifically to worship.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
To look early or diligently for. A primitive root; properly, to dawn, i.e. be early at any task; by extension, to search for.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
35: Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.

An elevation, lofty, as title, the Supreme. From alah; an elevation, i.e. Lofty; as title, the Supreme.
To redeem, act as kinsman. A primitive root, to redeem, i.e. To be the next of kin.
36: A primitive root; to open, i.e. Be roomy; usually figuratively to be simple or delude.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
To lie, be a liar. A primitive root; to lie, literally or figuratively.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
37: A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A covenant. From barah (like bara'); a compact.
38: A primitive root; to cover; figuratively, to expiate or condone, to placate or cancel.
Iniquity, guilt, punishment for iniquity. Or oavown; from avah; perversity, i.e. evil.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
39: Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.

Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.

God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.

Sacred, holy. Or qadosh; from qadash; sacred; God, an angel, a saint, a sanctuary.

42: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To ransom. A primitive root; to sever, i.e. Ransom; gener. To release, preserve.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
43: Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
A sign. Probably from 'uwth; a signal, as a flag, beacon, monument, omen, prodigy, evidence, etc.

Field, land. Or saday; from an unused root meaning to spread out; a field.

Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.

45: To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.

To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Frogs. From tsaphar and a word elsewhere unused meaning a swamp; a marsh-leaper, i.e. Frog.
Perhaps to go to ruin. A primitive root; to decay, i.e. ruin.
46: To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Produce (of the soil). From yabal; produce, i.e. A crop or wealth.
(a kind of) locust. From chacal; the ravager, i.e. A locust.
Toil, product. From yaga'; toil; hence, a work, produce, property.
(a kind of) locust. From rabah; a locust.

A vine. From an unused root meaning to bend; a vine, especially the grape.
Sycamore tree. Or shiqmah; of uncertain derivation; a sycamore.

Cattle. From qanah; something bought, i.e. Property, but only livestock; abstractly, acquisition.
Flame. From saraph; a live coal; by analogy lightning; figuratively, an arrow,; specifically, fever.

(burning of) anger. Or charon; from charah; a burning of anger.

Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
A messenger. From an unused root meaning to despatch as a deputy; a messenger; specifically, of God, i.e. An angel.

Path, pathway. Or nthiybah; or nthibah; from an unused root meaning to tramp; a track.

Death. From muwth; death; concretely, the dead, their place or state; figuratively, pestilence, ruin.

Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.

Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.

An elevation, lofty, as title, the Supreme. From alah; an elevation, i.e. Lofty; as title, the Supreme.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
Testimony, witness. Feminine of ed in its techn. Sense; testimony.
57: To move away, backslide. A primitive root; properly, to flinch, i.e. to go back, literally or figuratively.
To act or deal treacherously. A primitive root; to cover; figuratively, to act covertly; by implication, to pillage.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
To turn, overturn. A primitive root; to turn about or over; by implication, to change, overturn, return, pervert.
Remissness, treachery. From ramah; remissness, treachery.
A bow. From qashah in the original sense of bending: a bow, for shooting or the iris.
58: To be vexed or angry. A primitive root; to trouble; by implication, to grieve, rage, be indignant.
A high place. From an unused root; an elevation.
To be jealous or zealous. A primitive root; to be zealous, i.e. jealous or envious.
An idol, image. From pacal; an idol.
59: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
To spurn, to disappear. A primitive root; to spurn; also to disappear.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
Muchness, force, abundance. From the same as 'uwd; properly, vehemence, i.e. vehemently; by implication, wholly, speedily, etc.
60: A primitive root; properly, to pound, i.e. Smite; by implication to disperse; also, to thrust off, down, out or upon.
Dwelling place, tabernacle. From shakan; a residence; specifically, the Tabernacle.
A city in Ephraim. Or Shiloh; or Shiylow; or Shilow; from the same as Shiyloh; Shiloh, a place in Palestine.
A primitive root (by transmission) to shakab through the idea of lodging; compare cikluwth, shakam); to reside or permanently stay.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
61: To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.
Exiled, captured, exile, booty. From shbuw; exiled; captured; as noun, exile; by extension, booty.
Beauty, glory. Or tiphereth; from pa'ar; ornament.
Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
62: To shut, close. A primitive root; to shut up; figuratively, to surrender.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
From nachal; properly, something inherited, i.e. occupancy, or an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion.
63: A fire. A primitive word; fire.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
A young man. Or bachur; participle passive of bachar; properly, selected, i.e. A youth.
Feminine passive participle of an unused root meaning to separate; a virgin; sometimes a bride; also a city or state.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
64: Priest. Active participle of kahan; literally, one officiating, a priest; also an acting priest.
To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
A widow. Fem of 'alman; a widow; also a desolate place.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To weep, bewail. A primitive root; to weep; generally to bemoan.
65: Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
To awake. A primitive root; to awake.
Strong, mighty. Or gibbor; intensive from the same as geber; powerful; by implication, warrior, tyrant.
To give a ringing cry. A primitive root; properly, to creak, i.e. To shout.
Wine. From an unused root meaning to effervesce; wine; by implication, intoxication.
66: To smite. A primitive root; to strike.
The hind side, back part. Or achor; from Achiyra'; the hinder part; hence behind, backward; also the West.
Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
A reproach. From charaph. contumely, disgrace, the pudenda.
67: To spurn, to disappear. A primitive root; to spurn; also to disappear.
Future of yacaph; let him add; Joseph, the name of seven Israelites.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To choose. A primitive root; properly, to try, i.e. select.
Rod, staff, club, scepter, tribe. From an unused root probably meaning to branch off; a scion, i.e. a stick Or a clan.
Dual of masculine form of 'Ephraath; double fruit; Ephrajim, a son of Joseph; also the tribe descended from him, and its territory.
68: To choose. A primitive root; properly, to try, i.e. select.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Rod, staff, club, scepter, tribe. From an unused root probably meaning to branch off; a scion, i.e. a stick Or a clan.
From yadah; celebrated; Jehudah, the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
A mountain in Jer., also a name for Jer. The same as tsiyuwn; Tsijon, a mountain of Jerusalem.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
69: To build. A primitive root; to build.
A sacred place, sanctuary. Or miqqdash; from qadash; a consecrated thing or place, especially, a palace, sanctuary or asylum.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
To be high or exalted, rise. A primitive root; to be high actively, to rise or raise.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A primitive root; to set; intensively, to found; reflexively, to sit down together, i.e. Settle, consult.
70: To choose. A primitive root; properly, to try, i.e. select.
Perhaps beloved one, a son of Jesse. Rarely; Daviyd; from the same as dowd; loving; David, the youngest son of Jesse.
Slave, servant. From abad; a servant.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
An enclosure, fold. Or miklah; from kala'; a pen.
Small cattle, sheep and goats, flock. Or tsaown; from an unused root meaning to migrate; a collective name for a flock; also figuratively.
71: The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
To nurse, give suck. A primitive root; to suckle, i.e. Give milk.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
A primitive root; to tend a flock; i.e. Pasture it; intransitively, to graze; generally to rule; by extension, to associate with.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
A son of Isaac, also his desc. From aqab; heel-catcher; Jaakob, the Israelitish patriarch.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
From nachal; properly, something inherited, i.e. occupancy, or an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion.
72: A primitive root; to tend a flock; i.e. Pasture it; intransitively, to graze; generally to rule; by extension, to associate with.
From tamam; completeness; figuratively, prosperity; usually innocence.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
To lead, guide. A primitive root; to guide; by implication, to transport.
An understanding. And tbuwnah; or towbunah; from biyn; intelligence; by implication, an argument; by extension, caprice.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.

Gatherer, the name of several Isr. From 'acaph; collector; Asaph, the name of three Israelites, and of the family of the first.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.

To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
From nachal; properly, something inherited, i.e. occupancy, or an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion.

Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
A palace, temple. Probably from yakol; a large public building, such as a palace or temple.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Probably foundation of peace, capital city of all Isr.
A ruin, heap of ruins. From avah; a ruin.

Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.

Slave, servant. From abad; a servant.
Flying creatures. From uwph; a bird, often collectively.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.
Kind, pious. From chacad; properly, kind, i.e. pious.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
Probably foundation of peace, capital city of all Isr.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To bury. A primitive root; to intern.
4: To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A reproach. From charaph. contumely, disgrace, the pudenda.
Inhabitant, neighbor. From shakan; a resident; by extension, a fellow-citizen.
A mocking, derision. From la'ag; derision, scoffing.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
5: As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To be angry. A primitive root; to breathe hard, i.e. Be enraged.
Ardor, zeal, jealousy. From qana'; jealousy or envy.
To kindle, consume, to be, brutish. A primitive root; to kindle, i.e. Consume; also to be(-come) brutish.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.

Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

Kingdom, sovereignty, dominion, reign. From malak; dominion, i.e. the estate or the country.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

Or navah; from navah; at home; hence lovely; also a home, of God, men, flocks, or wild animals.
8: Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
Former, first, chief. Or riishon; from ri'shah; first, in place, time or rank.
Iniquity, guilt, punishment for iniquity. Or oavown; from avah; perversity, i.e. evil.
Compassion. From racham; compassion; by extension, the womb; by implication, a maiden.
To come or be in front, meet. A primitive root; to project, i.e. Precede; hence, to anticipate, hasten, meet.
Hastening, speedy, swift. From mahar; properly, hurrying; hence in a hurry.
To hang, be low, languish. A primitive root; to slacken or be feeble; figuratively, to be oppressed.
Muchness, force, abundance. From the same as 'uwd; properly, vehemence, i.e. vehemently; by implication, wholly, speedily, etc.
9: To help, succor. A primitive root; to surround, i.e. Protect or aid.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Deliverance, rescue, salvation, safety, welfare. Or yeshai; from yasha'; liberty, deliverance, prosperity.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
To strip, plunder, deliver oneself, be delivered, snatch away, deliver.
A primitive root; to cover; figuratively, to expiate or condone, to placate or cancel.

Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Arm, shoulder, strength. Or zroao; and zrowpah; or zrowah; from zara'; the arm, or the foreleg; figuratively, force.

Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).

Bosom. Or cheq; and chowq; from an unused root, apparently meaning to inclose; the bosom.
Inhabitant, neighbor. From shakan; a resident; by extension, a fellow-citizen.
Sevenfold, seven times. Dual of sheba'; seven- times.
A reproach. From charaph. contumely, disgrace, the pudenda.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To pull off, to expose, to betroth, to carp at, defame, to spend the winter.
Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.

A pasturing, shepherding, pasturage. From ra'ah in the sense of feeding; pasturage; concretely, a flock.
Period, generation, dwelling. Or dor; from duwr; properly, a revolution of time, i.e. An age or generation; also a dwelling.
Period, generation, dwelling. Or dor; from duwr; properly, a revolution of time, i.e. An age or generation; also a dwelling.
Praise, song of praise. From halal; laudation; specifically a hymn.

Or showshan; or shoshan; and showshannah; from suws; a lily, as a flower of arch. Ornament; also a trumpet: lily, Shoshannim.

Gatherer, the name of several Isr. From 'acaph; collector; Asaph, the name of three Israelites, and of the family of the first.

Strength, might. Feminine passive participle from the same as gibbowr; force; by implication, valor, victory.

Salvation. Feminine passive participle of yasha'; something saved, i.e. deliverance; hence, aid, victory, prosperity.

To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.

Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
When?. From an unused root meaning to extend; properly, extent; but used only adverbially, when.

Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.

Strife, contention. From diyn; a contest or quarrel.
Inhabitant, neighbor. From shakan; a resident; by extension, a fellow-citizen.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.

A vine. From an unused root meaning to bend; a vine, especially the grape.
A son of Ham, also his desc. and their country in N.W. Africa. Dual of matsowr; Mitsrajim, i.e. Upper and Lower Egypt.

A root. From sharash; a root.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
10: Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
To cover. A primitive root; properly, to plump, i.e. Fill up hollows; by implication, to cover.
A shadow. From tsalal; shade, whether literal or figurative.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.

From an unused root apparently meaning to be conspicuous; fulness of the breast; also a moving creature.
