Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
To look. A primitive root; to scan, i.e. Look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
To attend to, visit, muster, appoint. A primitive root; to visit; by analogy, to oversee, muster, charge, care for, miss, deposit, etc.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
A vine. From an unused root meaning to bend; a vine, especially the grape.
15: Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To plant. A primitive root; properly, to strike in, i.e. Fix; specifically, to plant.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
To be stout, strong, bold, alert. A primitive root; to be alert, physically or mentally.
16: Your vine has been cut down
To cut off or away. A primitive root; to cut off.
To burn. A primitive root; to be on fire.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
A rebuke. From ga'ar; a chiding.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
17: To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
To be stout, strong, bold, alert. A primitive root; to be alert, physically or mentally.
18: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To move away, backslide. A primitive root; properly, to flinch, i.e. to go back, literally or figuratively.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
To live, to revive. A primitive root; to live, whether literally or figuratively; causatively, to revive.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
19: A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To be or become light. A primitive root; to be luminous.
To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.

1: Preeminent or enduring. A primitive root; properly, to glitter from afar, i.e. To be eminent; also, to be permanent.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A musical term of unc. meaning. Feminine of Gittiy; a Gittite harp.
Gatherer, the name of several Isr. From 'acaph; collector; Asaph, the name of three Israelites, and of the family of the first.
To give a ringing cry. A primitive root; properly, to creak, i.e. To shout.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.
To raise a shout, give a blast. A primitive root; to mar; figuratively, to split the ears, i.e. Shout.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
A son of Isaac, also his desc. From aqab; heel-catcher; Jaakob, the Israelitish patriarch.
2: Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
Melody, song (in praise of Yah). From zamar; a musical piece or song to be accompanied by an instrument.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
A timbrel, tambourine. From taphaph contracted; a tambourine.
A skin-bag for, liquids, a vase, a lyre. Or nebel; from nabel; a skin-bag for liquids; hence, a vase; also a lyre.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
A lyre. From a unused root meaning to twang; a harp.
3: A primitive root; to clatter, i.e. Slap, clang; by analogy, to drive; by implication, to become bondsman by handclasping).
A horn (for blowing). Or shophar; from shaphar in the original sense of incising; a cornet or curved horn.
New moon, a month. From chadash; the new moon; by implication, a month.
Full moon. Or keceh; apparently from kacah; properly, fulness or the full moon, i.e. Its festival.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
A festival gathering, feast, pilgrim feast. Or chag; from chagag; a festival, or a victim therefor.
4: Something prescribed or owed, a statute. From chaqaq; an enactment; hence, an appointment.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
A son of Isaac, also his desc. From aqab; heel-catcher; Jaakob, the Israelitish patriarch.
5: To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Jehoseph. A fuller form of Yowceph; Jehoseph, a son of Jacob.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A son of Ham, also his desc. and their country in N.W. Africa. Dual of matsowr; Mitsrajim, i.e. Upper and Lower Egypt.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
6: To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
Shoulder. From shakam; the neck as the place of burdens; figuratively, the spur of a hill.
A load, burden. From cabal; a load.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
A pot, jar. From the same as dowd; a pot; also a basket.
7: To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
A primitive root; to pull off; hence to strip, To depart; by implication, to deliver, equip; present, strengthen.
A covering, hiding place, secrecy. Or cithrah,; from cathar; a cover.
Thunder. From ra'am; a peal of thunder.
To examine, try. A primitive root; to test; generally and figuratively, to investigate.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Place of strife, two places in the desert. The same as mriybah; Meribah, the name of two places in the Desert.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
8: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
A primitive root; to duplicate or repeat; by implication, to protest, testify; intensively, to encompass, restore.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
9: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A primitive root; to turn aside; hence to be a foreigner, strange, profane; specifically to commit adultery.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To bow down. A primitive root; to depress, i.e. Prostrate.
That which is foreign, foreignness. From nakar; foreign, or a foreigner, or heathendom.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
10: I. Sometimes; a primitive pro. I.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A son of Ham, also his desc. and their country in N.W. Africa. Dual of matsowr; Mitsrajim, i.e. Upper and Lower Egypt.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
To be or grow wide or large. A primitive root; to broaden.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
11: From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

Firmness, stubbornness. From sharar in the sense of twisted, i.e. Firm; obstinacy.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Counsel, plan, principle, device. From ya'ats; a purpose.
13: If, oh that. Or lu; or luw; a conditional particle; if; by implication would that!

Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
14: A little, fewness, a few. Or miat; from ma'at; a little or few.
To be humble. A primitive root; properly, to bend the knee; hence, to humiliate, vanquish.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.

To disappoint, deceive, fail, grow lean. A primitive root; to be untrue, in word or deed.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
16: To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Or cheleb; from an unused root meaning to be fat; fat, whether literally or figuratively; hence, the richest or choice part.
Wheat. Of uncertain derivation; wheat, whether the grain or the plant.
Honey. From an unused root meaning to be gummy; honey; by analogy, syrup.
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
To be sated, satisfied or surfeited. Or sabeay; a primitive root; to sate, i.e. Fill to satisfaction.

When?. From an unused root meaning to extend; properly, extent; but used only adverbially, when.
Injustice, unrighteousness. Or lavel; and lavlah; or owlah; or.olah; from aval; evil.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.

Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.

An orphan. From an unused root meaning to be lonely; a bereaved person.
To be just or righteous. A primitive root; to be right.
Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.

Darkness. Or chashekah; from chashak; darkness; figuratively, misery.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To totter, shake, slip. A primitive root; to waver; by implication, to slip, shake, fall.
6: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
Chieftain, chief, ruler, official, captain, prince. From sarar; a head person.
To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
8: To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.

Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.

From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.

Arm, shoulder, strength. Or zroao; and zrowpah; or zrowah; from zara'; the arm, or the foreleg; figuratively, force.

The same as midyan; Midjan, a son of Abraham; also his country and his descendants.

Or nachlah; or nachalah; from nachal in its original sense; a stream, especially a winter torrent; a valley; also a shaft.
A wadi in the plain of Megiddo. From qowsh; winding; Kishon, a river of Palestine.

A Midianite. Or mowreb; the same as oreb; Oreb, the name of a Midianite and of the cliff near the Jordan.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Or yaresh; a primitive root; to occupy; by implication, to seize, to rob, to inherit; also to expel, to impoverish, to ruin.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.

13: To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
A wheel, whirl, whirlwind. By reduplication from galal; a wheel; by analogy, a whirlwind; also dust.

Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
14: A fire. A primitive word; fire.
To kindle, consume, to be, brutish. A primitive root; to kindle, i.e. Consume; also to be(-come) brutish.
From an unused root probably meaning to thicken with verdure; a copse of bushes; hence, a forest; hence, honey in the comb.
Flame, blade. Or lahebeth; feminine of lahab, and meaning the same.
To blaze up, flame. A primitive root; properly, to lick, i.e. to blaze.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
15: So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
To pursue, chase, persecute. A primitive root; to run after (of time) gone by).
A hurricane. Or ctarah; from ca'ar; a hurricane.
To disturb, terrify. A primitive root; to tremble inwardly, i.e. be alarmed or agitated; by implication to hasten anxiously.
A storm wind. From cuwph; a hurricane.

Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Ignominy, dishonor. From qalah; disgrace; the pudenda.
To seek. A primitive root; to search out; by implication, to strive after.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
17: To be ashamed. A primitive root; properly, to pale, i.e. By implication to be ashamed; also to be disappointed or delayed.
Perpetuity. From adah; properly, a terminus, i.e. duration, in the sense of advance or perpetuity.
To disturb, terrify. A primitive root; to tremble inwardly, i.e. be alarmed or agitated; by implication to hasten anxiously.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
A primitive root: to blush; figuratively, to be ashamed, disappointed; causatively, to shame, reproach.
18: A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
An elevation, lofty, as title, the Supreme. From alah; an elevation, i.e. Lofty; as title, the Supreme.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A musical term of unc. meaning. Feminine of Gittiy; a Gittite harp.
A melody. From zamar; properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
An Edomite name, also an Isr. name. From qarach; ice; Korach, the name of two Edomites and three Israelites.
Beloved. From the same as dowd; loved.
Dwelling place, tabernacle. From shakan; a residence; specifically, the Tabernacle.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
2: From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
To long (for). A primitive root; properly, to become pale, i.e. to pine after; also to fear.
To be complete, at an end, finished, accomplished, or spent. A primitive root; to end, whether intransitive or transitived.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.
To give a ringing cry. A primitive root; properly, to creak, i.e. To shout.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
3: A bird. Or tsippor; from tsaphar; a little bird.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Swallow (a kind of bird). The same as drowr, applied to a bird; the swift, a kind of swallow.
A nest. Contracted from qanan; a nest, sometimes including the nestlings; figuratively, a chamber or dwelling.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
A young one. From parach; the brood of a bird.
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
An altar. From zabach; an altar.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
4: Happiness, blessedness. From 'ashar; happiness; only in masculine plural construction as interjection, how happy!
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
5: Happiness, blessedness. From 'ashar; happiness; only in masculine plural construction as interjection, how happy!
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
A highway. From calal; a thoroughfare, literally or figuratively; specifically a viaduct, a staircase.
6: To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
A vale. From amaq; a vale.
A valley in Pal. From bakah, weeping; Baca, a valley in Palestine.
To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
A spring. Or ma ynow; or ma yanah; from ayin; a fountain, figuratively, a source.
An archer, teacher, teaching, the early rain. From yarah; an archer; also teacher or teaching; also the early rain.
To wrap oneself, enwrap, envelop oneself. A primitive root; to wrap, i.e. Cover, veil, cloth, or roll.
A blessing. From barak; benediction; by implication prosperity.
7: To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
From chuwl; probably a force, whether of men, means or other resources; an army, wealth, virtue, valor, strength.

To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.

A shield. Also feminine mginnah; from ganan; a shield; figuratively, a protector; also the scaly hide of the crocodile.

To look. A primitive root; to scan, i.e. Look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Anointed. From mashach; anointed; usually a consecrated person; specifically, the Messiah.
10: Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
A thousand. Prop, the same as 'eleph; hence a thousand.
To choose. A primitive root; properly, to try, i.e. select.
A primitive root; properly, to snatch away, i.e. Terminate; but used only as denominative from caph, to wait at the threshold.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.

To heap up, pile, dwell. A primitive root; properly, to gyrate, i.e. To remain.

From an unused root meaning to be brilliant; the sun; by implication, the east; figuratively, a ray, i.e. A notched battlement.

To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Favor, grace. From chanan; graciousness, i.e. Subjective or objective.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
To withhold, hold back. A primitive root; to debar from benefit or injury.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

Complete, sound. From tamam; entire; also integrity, truth.

Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
A maid, handmaid. Apparently a primitive word; a maid-servant or female slave.
17: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
A sign. Probably from 'uwth; a signal, as a flag, beacon, monument, omen, prodigy, evidence, etc.
To hate. A primitive root; to hate.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
To be ashamed. A primitive root; properly, to pale, i.e. By implication to be ashamed; also to be disappointed or delayed.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To help, succor. A primitive root; to surround, i.e. Protect or aid.
A primitive root; properly, to sigh, i.e. Breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. to pity, console or rue; or to avenge.

1: A melody. From zamar; properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
An Edomite name, also an Isr. name. From qarach; ice; Korach, the name of two Edomites and three Israelites.
A song, singing. Or feminine shiyrah; from shiyr; a song; abstractly, singing.
Foundation. Feminine of ycud; a foundation.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
Hill, mountain. From an unused root meaning to loom up; a mountain.
2: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
A gate. From sha'ar in its original sense; an opening, i.e. Door or gate.
A mountain in Jer., also a name for Jer. The same as tsiyuwn; Tsijon, a mountain of Jerusalem.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Dwelling place, tabernacle. From shakan; a residence; specifically, the Tabernacle.
A son of Isaac, also his desc. From aqab; heel-catcher; Jaakob, the Israelitish patriarch.
3: Or kabed; a primitive root; to be heavy, i.e. In a bad sense or in a good sense (in the same two senses).
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
4: Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
Storm, a sea monster. From rahab, bluster(-er).
From balal; confusion; Babel, including Babylonia and the Babylonian empire.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
A territory on the S. Mediterranean coast of Isr. From palash; rolling, i.e. Migratory; Pelesheth, a region of Syria.
A Phoenician city. Or Tsowr; the same as tsor; a rock; Tsor, a place in Palestine.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
Probably of foreign origin; Cush, the name of a son of Ham, and of his territory; also of an Israelite.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
A primitive root; to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage.
There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
5: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
A mountain in Jer., also a name for Jer. The same as tsiyuwn; Tsijon, a mountain of Jerusalem.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
A primitive root; to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage.

A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
6: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To write. A primitive root; to grave, by implication, to write.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
A primitive root; to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage.
There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
7: To sing. Or shuwr; a primitive root; to sing.
Or chiyl; a primitive root; properly, to twist or whirl, i.e. to dance, to writhe in pain or fear; figuratively, to wait, to pervert.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A spring. Or ma ynow; or ma yanah; from ayin; a fountain, figuratively, a source.

Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.

An Israelite name. Probably from 'aman; faithful; Heman, the name of at least two Israelites.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.
To cry, cry out, call. A primitive root; to shriek; to proclaim.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
2: Prayer. From palal; intercession, supplication; by implication, a hymn.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
A primitive root; to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away; used in a great variety of application.
An ear. From 'azan; broadness. I.e. the ear.
A ringing cry. From ranan; properly, a creaking, i.e. Shout.
3: From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
To be sated, satisfied or surfeited. Or sabeay; a primitive root; to sate, i.e. Fill to satisfaction.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
A primitive root; properly, to touch, i.e. Lay the hand upon; by implication, to reach; violently, to strike.
Or shol; from sha'al; Hades or the world of the dead, including its accessories and inmates.

To come or go down, descend.
A pit, cistern, well. From buwr; a pit hole.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Man. From gabar; properly, a valiant man or warrior; generally, a person simply.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
5: To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
Pierced, polluted. From chalal; pierced; figuratively, polluted.
To lie down. A primitive root; to lie down.
A grave, sepulcher. Or qibrah; from qabar; a sepulchre.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
To cut, divide. A primitive root; to cut down or off; to destroy, divide, exclude, or decide.
6: To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
Lower, lowest. From tachath; lowermost; as noun the depths.
A pit, cistern, well. From buwr; a pit hole.
Dark place. From chashak; darkness; concretely, a dark place.
Depth, deep. Or mtsolah; also mtsuwlah; or mtsulah; from the same as tsuwlah; a deep place.
7: Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
To lean, lay, rest, support. A primitive root; to prop; reflexively, to lean upon or take hold of.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

To be bowed down or afflicted. A primitive root; to depress literally or figuratively, transitive or intransitive.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.

A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.

Affliction, poverty. From anah; depression, i.e. Misery.

To spread, spread abroad. A primitive root; to expand.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
10: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
A wonder. From pala'; a miracle.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
Shades, ghosts. From rapha' in the sense of raphah; properly, lax, i.e. a ghost.

From an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant; strictly nothingness; also trouble. Vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
Quickly. From chiysh; properly, a hurry; hence quickly.
To pass over or away. A primitive root; properly, to shear off; but used only in the sense of passing rapidly.
To fly, to faint. A primitive root; to cover; hence to fly; also to faint.
11: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.

A fear. Feminine of yare'; fear; morally, reverence.

As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.

Or liyn; a primitive root; to stop; by implication, to stay permanently; hence to be obstinate.

A, sheet, a spring net. From pachach; a sheet; also a spring net.

Wing, extremity. From kanaph; an edge or extremity; specifically a wing, a flap, a quarter, a pinnacle.
To seek refuge. A primitive root; to flee for protection; figuratively, to confide in.

Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.
From chatsats; properly, a piercer, i.e. An arrow; by implication, a wound; figuratively, thunder-bolt; the shaft of a spear.
To fly, to faint. A primitive root; to cover; hence to fly; also to faint.

Destruction. From an unused root meaning to cut off; ruin.
A primitive root; properly, to swell up, i.e. Figuratively to devastate.

To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
A side. Contr. From an unused root meaning to sidle off; a side; figuratively, an adversary.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Requital, retribution. Feminine of shilluwm; retribution.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.

High, exalted. Or gabowahh; from gabahh; elevated, powerful, arrogant.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
Wide, broad. From rachab; roomy, in any direction, literally or figuratively.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Complete, sound. From tamam; entire; also integrity, truth.
To minister, serve. A primitive root; to attend as a menial or worshipper; figuratively, to contribute to.

To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Remissness, treachery. From ramah; remissness, treachery.

Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
Deception, disappointment, falsehood. From shaqar; an untruth; by implication, a sham.

From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.

Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.

From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
10: A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
and in the shadow of death,
Death-like shadow, deep shadow. From tsel and maveth; shade of death, i.e. The grave.
A bondman, prisoner. From 'acar; bound, i.e. A captive.
Affliction, poverty. From anah; depression, i.e. Misery.
Iron. Perhaps from the root of Birzowth; iron; by extension, an iron implement.
11: To be contentious or rebellious. A primitive root; to be bitter; to rebel.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
To spurn, treat with contempt. A primitive root; to scorn; or by interchange for nuwts, to bloom.
Counsel, advice. From ya'ats; advice; by implication, plan; also prudence.
An elevation, lofty, as title, the Supreme. From alah; an elevation, i.e. Lofty; as title, the Supreme.
12: To be humble. A primitive root; properly, to bend the knee; hence, to humiliate, vanquish.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
Trouble, labor, toil. From amal; toil, i.e. Wearing effort; hence, worry, wheth. Of body or mind.
To stumble, stagger, totter. A primitive root; to totter or waver; by implication, to falter, stumble, faint or fall.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To help, succor. A primitive root; to surround, i.e. Protect or aid.
13: To cry, cry out, call. A primitive root; to shriek; by analogy, to announce or convene publicly.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.
Straits, stress. Or mtsuqah; feminine of matsuwq; narrowness, i.e. trouble.
14: A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
Death-like shadow, deep shadow. From tsel and maveth; shade of death, i.e. The grave.
A band, bond. Also feminine mowcerah; or mocrah; from yacar; properly, chastisement, i.e. a halter; figuratively, restraint.
To pull, draw, or tear away, apart, or off. A primitive root; to tear off.
15: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.
A primitive root; properly, perhaps to separate, i.e. Distinguish; by implication, to be great, difficult, wonderful.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.

A door. From dalah; something swinging, i.e. The valve of a door.
Copper, bronze. For nchuwshah; copper, hence, something made of that metal, i.e. Coin, a fetter; figuratively, base.
To hew, hew down or off. A primitive root; to fell a tree; generally, to destroy anything.
A bar. From barach; a bolt.
Iron. Perhaps from the root of Birzowth; iron; by extension, an iron implement.
17: Foolish. From an unused root; silly.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Iniquity, guilt, punishment for iniquity. Or oavown; from avah; perversity, i.e. evil.
To be bowed down or afflicted. A primitive root; to depress literally or figuratively, transitive or intransitive.
18: From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
To abhor. A primitive root; to loathe, i.e. detest.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A primitive root; properly, to touch, i.e. Lay the hand upon; by implication, to reach; violently, to strike.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
A gate. From sha'ar in its original sense; an opening, i.e. Door or gate.
Death. From muwth; death; concretely, the dead, their place or state; figuratively, pestilence, ruin.

Straits, stress. Or mtsuqah; feminine of matsuwq; narrowness, i.e. trouble.
20: To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
To heal. Or raphah; a primitive root; properly, to mend, i.e. to cure.
A primitive root; properly, to be smooth, i.e. to escape; causatively, to release or rescue; specifically, to bring forth young, emit sparks.
A pit. From shachah; a pit-fall.
21: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.

Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).

From yadah; properly, an extension of the hand, i.e. avowal, or adoration; specifically, a choir of worshippers.
A deed, work. From asah; an action; generally, a transaction; abstractly, activity; by implication, a product or property.
A ringing cry. From ranan; properly, a creaking, i.e. Shout.

A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
A ship. Feminine of 'oniy; a ship.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
From the same as mal'ak; properly, deputyship, i.e. Ministry; generally, employment or work; also property.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
24: They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
A deed, work. From asah; an action; generally, a transaction; abstractly, activity; by implication, a product or property.

A primitive root; properly, perhaps to separate, i.e. Distinguish; by implication, to be great, difficult, wonderful.
Depth, deep. Or mtsolah; also mtsuwlah; or mtsulah; from the same as tsuwlah; a deep place.

To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
A hurricane. Or ctarah; from ca'ar; a hurricane.
To be high or exalted, rise. A primitive root; to be high actively, to rise or raise.
A heap, wave, billow. From galal; something rolled, i.e. A heap of stone or dung, by analogy, a spring of water.
26: A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
To come or go down, descend.
Deep, sea, abyss. Or thom; from huwm; an abyss, especially the deep.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
To melt. A primitive root; to melt, i.e. Literally, or figuratively.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

Beginning, chief. From the same as ro'sh; the first, in place, time, order or rank.

To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Prudence, insight. Or sekel; from sakal; intelligence; by implication, success.
Praise, song of praise. From halal; laudation; specifically a hymn.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
Perpetuity. From adah; properly, a terminus, i.e. duration, in the sense of advance or perpetuity.

1: The name of the God of Israel. Contraction for Yhovah, and meaning the same; Jah, the sacred name.
Happiness, blessedness. From 'ashar; happiness; only in masculine plural construction as interjection, how happy!
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
Fearing, reverent. From yare'; fearing; morally, reverent.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Muchness, force, abundance. From the same as 'uwd; properly, vehemence, i.e. vehemently; by implication, wholly, speedily, etc.
A primitive root; properly, to incline to; by implication to bend; figuratively, to be pleased with, desire.
Commandment. From tsavah; a command, whether human or divine.
2: A sowing, seed, offspring. From zara'; seed; figuratively, fruit, plant, sowing-time, posterity.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Strong, mighty. Or gibbor; intensive from the same as geber; powerful; by implication, warrior, tyrant.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Period, generation, dwelling. Or dor; from duwr; properly, a revolution of time, i.e. An age or generation; also a dwelling.
To kneel, bless. A primitive root; to kneel; by implication to bless God, and man; also to curse.
3: Wealth, sufficiency. From the same as huwn in the sense of 'own; wealth; by implication, enough.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
Perpetuity. From adah; properly, a terminus, i.e. duration, in the sense of advance or perpetuity.
4: A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
To rise, come forth. A primitive root; properly, to irradiate, i.e. To rise; specifically, to appear.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
5: Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
A primitive root; properly, to bend or stoop in kindness to an inferior; to favor, bestow; causatively to implore.
A primitive root; properly, to twine, i.e. to unite, to remain; also to borrow or To lend.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
To comprehend, contain. A primitive root; properly, to keep in; hence, to measure; figuratively, to maintain.
6: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To totter, shake, slip. A primitive root; to waver; by implication, to slip, shake, fall.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Remembrance, memorial. Or zeker; from zakar; a memento, abstr. Recollection; by implication, commemoration.
7: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
A report. Feminine passive participle of shamem; something heard, i.e. An announcement.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
8: A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To lean, lay, rest, support. A primitive root; to prop; reflexively, to lean upon or take hold of.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
9: To scatter. A primitive root; to scatter, whether in enmity or bounty.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
In want, needy, poor. From 'abah, in the sense of want; destitute.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
Perpetuity. From adah; properly, a terminus, i.e. duration, in the sense of advance or perpetuity.
From qaran; a horn; by implication, a flask, cornet; by resembl. An elephant's tooth, a corner, a peak, a ray; figuratively, power.
To be high or exalted, rise. A primitive root; to be high actively, to rise or raise.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
10: Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
To be vexed or angry. A primitive root; to trouble; by implication, to grieve, rage, be indignant.
To gnash or grind. A primitive root; to grate the teeth.
Tooth, ivory. From shanan; a tooth; specifically ivory; figuratively, a cliff.
To dissolve, melt. A primitive root; to liquefy; figuratively, to waste, to faint.
A desire. From 'avah; a longing; by implication, a delight.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.

To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.

Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

Dangling, weak, thin. From dalal; properly, dangling, i.e. weak or thin.

A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.

If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!

Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.

A primitive root; to pull off; hence to strip, To depart; by implication, to deliver, equip; present, strengthen.

Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.

Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
5: Happiness, blessedness. From 'ashar; happiness; only in masculine plural construction as interjection, how happy!
A help, helper. From azar; aid.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.

Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.

To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.

Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.

8: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To open, to be observant. A primitive root; to open; figuratively, to be observant.

To raise up. A primitive root; to life, i.e. comfort.
To bend, bend down, be bent or bowed. A primitive root; to curve.

To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.

Number, tally. From caphar; a number, definite or indefinite; also narration.
A star. Probably from the same as Kabbown or kavah; a star; figuratively, a prince.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.

Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.

Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.

Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.

To kneel, bless. A primitive root; to kneel; by implication to bless God, and man; also to curse.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).

Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.

Wool. From an unused root probably meaning to be shaggy; wool.

Frost, ice. Or qorach; from qarach; ice; hence, hail; by resemblance, rock crystal.
Fragment, bit, morsel. From pathath; a bit.

Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.

Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.

To blow. A primitive root; to blow; by implication, to disperse.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To flow, trickle, drop, distill. A primitive root; to drip, or shed by trickling.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
Not. From balah; properly, a failure; by implication nothing; usually not at all; also lest.

And towkachath; from yakach; chastisement; figuratively correction, refutation, proof.
Not. From balah; properly, a failure; by implication nothing; usually not at all; also lest.
A tribe, people. From the same as 'em; a collection, i.e. Community of persons.
8: To tie, bind, imprison. A primitive root; to yoke or hitch; by analogy, to fasten in any sense, to join battle.
(Isa. 'Abiyshuwae:; and ziq; or zeq; from zanaq; properly, what leaps forth, i.e. Flash of fire, or a burning arrow; also a bond.
Or kabed; a primitive root; to be heavy, i.e. In a bad sense or in a good sense (in the same two senses).
A fetter. From an unused root meaning to twine or braid together; a fetter.
Iron. Perhaps from the root of Birzowth; iron; by extension, an iron implement.
9: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
To write. A primitive root; to grave, by implication, to write.
An ornament, honor, splendor. From hadar; magnificence, i.e. Ornament or splendor.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Kind, pious. From chacad; properly, kind, i.e. pious.
The name of the God of Israel. Contraction for Yhovah, and meaning the same; Jah, the sacred name.

1: The name of the God of Israel. Contraction for Yhovah, and meaning the same; Jah, the sacred name.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.
An extended surface, expanse. From raqa'; properly, an expanse, i.e. The firmament or visible arch of the sky.
2: Strength, might. Feminine passive participle from the same as gibbowr; force; by implication, valor, victory.
Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.
3: Blast (of a horn). From taqa'; a blast of a trumpet.
A horn (for blowing). Or shophar; from shaphar in the original sense of incising; a cornet or curved horn.
A skin-bag for, liquids, a vase, a lyre. Or nebel; from nabel; a skin-bag for liquids; hence, a vase; also a lyre.
A lyre. From a unused root meaning to twang; a harp.
4: A timbrel, tambourine. From taphaph contracted; a tambourine.
A dance. From chuwl; a dance.
String (of a harp). From an unused root meaning to apportion; a part; hence, a musical chord.
Or buggab; from agab in the original sense of breathing; a reed-instrument of music.
5: A hearing, report. From shama'; something heard, i.e. A sound, rumor, announcement; abstractly, audience.
From tsalal reduplicated; a clatter, i.e. whirring; a cricket; also a harpoon, a cymbal.
From ruwa'; clamor, i.e. Acclamation of joy or a battle-cry; especially clangor of trumpets, as an alarum.
From tsalal reduplicated; a clatter, i.e. whirring; a cricket; also a harpoon, a cymbal.
6: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Breath. From nasham; a puff, i.e. Wind, angry or vital breath, divine inspiration, intellect. Or an animal.
The name of the God of Israel. Contraction for Yhovah, and meaning the same; Jah, the sacred name.
